1.title: Andy’s Computer Problems
paras: Darn it! My computer froze again.
Why does it keep doing that?
Someone sent a file to my email that had a virus attached to it and I opened it.
So your computer keeps freezing?
Yeah, but that’s not all. Windows pop up repeatedly, programs close without notice, and files seem to be disappearing.
Sounds like your computer has a mind of its own.
You can say that again. But what can I do?
I think you need to invest in some anti-virus software.
Man, that is such a scam. You know it is the people who sell the software that make the viruses, don’t you?
Maybe, but what choice do you have?
I’ll just reformat my hard drive and reinstall the operating system. That’ll fix it.
Wouldn’t it be easier just to buy the software?
Possibly, but this way is more reliable. After that I’ll just have to be more careful opening things that people send to me.
Suit yourself.

1.translation: 安迪：真要命，我的電腦又當機了。 湯尼：為什麼它會一直當機呢？ 安迪：有人寄了一封夾帶病毒檔案的郵件到我的信箱中，而且我把它打開來看。 湯尼：所以你的電腦就一直當機了嗎？ 安迪：是啊！但不只是這樣，視窗不斷地彈出，程式在無預警下就關閉，而且檔案似乎一直消失。 湯尼：聽起來就像是你的電腦有自我意志一樣。 安迪：我完全同意，但我能做什麼呢？ 湯尼：我認為你需要花錢買些防毒軟體。 安迪：天啊！那真是一個騙局。你知道電腦病毒是賣防毒軟體的人作出來的，不是嗎？ 湯尼：或許是吧，但你還有什麼選擇嗎？ 安迪：我只要把我的硬碟重新格式化，並且重新安裝作業系統，那就能修好了。 湯尼：那購買防毒軟體豈不是更加容易嗎？ 安迪：也許是吧，但是這樣比較可靠。以後只需在開啟別人寄給我的檔案時小心一點就好了。 湯尼：隨你便吧！
answers: It keeps freezing up.
He opened an email that had a virus attached to it.
Tony thinks Andy should buy anti-virus software.
paras_zh: 該死！我的電腦又當機了。
有人寄了一個附加病毒的檔案到我的信箱，我把它打開了。
所以你的電腦一直當機？
對啊，而且不只這樣。視窗會不停跳出來，程式無預警就關閉，檔案好像也一直在消失。
聽起來你的電腦好像有自己的想法。
一點沒錯。但我該怎麼辦？
我覺得你需要買一套防毒軟體來投資一下。
天啊，那根本是個騙局。你知道賣軟體的人其實就是製造病毒的人吧？
或許吧，但你還有別的選擇嗎？
我乾脆重新格式化硬碟，再重新安裝作業系統，這樣就能解決了。
買軟體不是比較簡單嗎？
也許吧，但這樣做比較可靠。之後我打開別人寄給我的東西時也會更小心。

quiz.q: Why does Andy think anti-virus software is a scam?
options: Because it is too expensive for him to afford.
Because he believes the people who sell it are the same people who make the viruses.
Because it slowed down his computer even more.
Because Tony told him it does not work at all.

quiz.q: What does Andy decide to do instead of just buying anti-virus software?
options: Ask Tony to fix his computer for him.
Reformat his hard drive and reinstall the operating system.

quiz.q: How does Andy plan to change his behavior after this experience?
options: He plans to be more careful about opening things people send him.
He plans to stop using email altogether.
He plans to buy a completely new computer every year.
He plans to only use his phone instead of his computer.

1.title: The Handy Backscratcher
paras: Can you give me a hand with something?
Sure. (She begins clapping.)
Oh thanks, you are hilarious.
Okay. Okay. What do you want help with?
I have a really itchy back and I can’t reach it to scratch. Can you scratch it for me?
Yeah, right! I wouldn’t touch you with a ten-foot pole. But I do happen to have a backscratcher in my backpack. (She hands him the backscratcher.) Here you go.
(He grabs the backscratcher and starts scratching his back like a mad man.) Thanks. That feels much better.
You owe me one. Remember the old saying: You scratch my back and I’ll scratch yours.
Right. I won’t forget.
You’d better not forget, if you know what’s good for you.  Questions: What is the backscratcher made from? Why is Eric looking for help? Does Coco want to scratch his itch for him? Why not?

1.translation: 這是一支竹子做的長柄扒。它有一支細長扁平的柄，而且一邊的末端是彎曲的。彎曲的這一端被做成像手一樣的形狀，對於那些很癢卻很難觸及的部位，這種設計是很棒的，像是背部中間的癢處。 艾瑞克正在尋求幫忙，他背部很癢，但是他就是抓不到。他發現蔻蔻就坐在長椅上，他想知道她是否可以幫他的忙。 對話艾瑞克：妳可以用幫我做件事嗎？ (註：give sb. a hand一語雙關，它除了幫忙之外，還有拍手的意思) 蔻蔻：當然。（她開始拍手鼓掌） 艾瑞克：哦，謝謝，妳很好笑耶！ 蔻蔻：好啦，好啦，你想要我幫你什麼忙呢？ 艾瑞克：我背部很癢，但是我就是抓不到。妳可以幫我抓一下嗎？ 蔻蔻：是啊，沒錯！就算用十呎長的竿子，我也不會想要幫你抓癢。但是我的背包裡剛好有一支長柄扒。（她把長柄扒遞給他）給你。 艾瑞克：（他拿起長柄扒，像個瘋子似的開始抓他背部的癢處。）多謝。感覺好多了。 蔻蔻：你欠我一個人情。記得俗話說：你幫我的背部抓癢，我就會幫你的背部抓癢(註：人應當彼此互相幫忙)。 艾瑞克：是的，我不會忘記的。 蔻蔻：你最好不要忘記，如果你知道怎麼做對你才是好的。  Answers: It is made from bamboo. He is looking for help because he has an itch on his back that he can’t reach. No, she doesn’t because she doesn’t want to touch him.

1.title: Sharing
paras: Umm! This chocolate bar is so good.
Can I have some?
No way! You never share with me when you have a treat.
That’s because you’re a pig and always take too much.
I am not a pig. You are!
(Starts to cry.) You are so mean. I am your little sister and you should take care of 5me.
Okay, okay. Stop your crying. Here you go. Grace hands her a piece of chocolate.
You are the best sister ever.
Remember that the next time I want some of your candy.
I will. Don’t worry.

1.translation: 葛芮絲(姊姊) 朱莉(妹妹) 葛芮絲：嗯！這個巧克力棒真好吃。 朱莉：可以給我一些嗎? 葛芮絲：想都別想！當你有好吃的東西時，你從來沒有和我分享過。 朱莉：那是因為你是隻豬，總是拿走好多。 葛芮絲：我不是豬，你才是! 朱莉：(開始哭泣) 你好吝嗇，我是你的妹妹，你應該要照顧我啊。 葛芮絲：好啦，好啦，你不要哭了，這些給你。 格雷斯給她一些巧克力。 朱莉：你是最好的姊姊。 葛芮絲：記得下次要給我一些你的糖果。 朱莉：我會給你的，別擔心。
answers: She starts to cry because her big sister is being mean to her.
Because Grace finally gives her a piece of the chocolate.

1.title: Moon Festival BBQ
paras: What did you do for the Moon Festival?
I went to my parent’s house for a BBQ.
How was that?
It was great. My mother prepared a lot of different foods for us to eat.
Is your mother a good cook?
Yes, she is, but she only prepared the food. My father actually did the cooking on the BBQ as he always does. He thinks he is the best.
That’s funny! My dad is the same way. He thinks he is the only one that can cook on a BBQ.
Yeah. I think everyone’s dad thinks that they are the best BBQ-er.
True, but it is always the moms left to clean up the mess.

1.translation: 喬：你中秋節時做了什麼?泰德：我去我父母的家裡烤肉。喬：烤得如何呢?泰德：很棒。我媽媽準備了很多不同的食物給我們吃。喬：你媽媽很會做菜嗎?泰德：是的，沒錯，但她只是準備食物，實際上負責烤肉的人是我爸爸，這一向如此。他認為他最會烤肉了。喬：那真是有趣，我爸爸也是一樣的，他認為他是唯一會烤肉的人。泰德：是啊。我認為每個人的爸爸都認為他們是最棒的烤肉高手。喬：沒錯！不過留下來收拾殘局的人總是媽媽。
answers: He went to his parent’s house for a BBQ.
Ted’s father did the cooking.
They both think that they are the best at cooking on the BBQ.

1.title: Buying Bread
paras: I love the smell of a bakery.
Me, too. Every time I come here, my mouth starts to water as soon as I walk through the door. So, what do you need to get?
I need to buy some bread so that I can make a sandwich for my lunch tomorrow. Where is it?
I think the bread is over by the pastries. They find the area where the bread is.
Wow. There are so many different kinds of bread. It makes it hard to choose.
What is this long one?
That is garlic bread. It has butter and garlic already inside. You heat it up in the oven and then slice it. It is usually served with steak, pasta or Caesar salad.
I think I’ve had it before, but I don’t really like garlic that much. What are these ones that look like a bull’s horns?

They are called croissants. They are very popular for breakfast. They are usually heated and then you can put some butter on them. Sometimes, people even slice them in half and make sandwiches. Tuna salad and crab salad croissants are both delicious and great light lunches.

Look here! You can even get hamburger and hot dog buns here. They are quite hard to find in Taiwan. Maybe I should pick up a dozen hot dog buns and we can have a wiener roast at the beach this weekend.
That sounds like a great idea. Jack takes a couple loaves of bread off the shelf.
Is that all you need?
Yep. One loaf of white bread and one whole-wheat should get me through the week.
Alright. We can pay and go home.
On second thought, maybe I should grab a half a dozen doughnuts…as long as we are here.
I knew you couldn’t resist buying something sweet.
You know me too well.

1.translation: 傑克和他最好的朋友艾琳在鎮上另一頭的麵包店。傑克：我最喜歡麵包的味道了。艾琳：我也是。每次我來到這裡，一走進大門後，我的嘴巴就開始流口水。那麼，你要買什麼呢？傑克：我要買一些麵包，這樣我明天就可以做三明治當午餐。它在哪裡呢？艾琳：我想麵包在那邊，就在糕餅區的旁邊。他們找到了麵包區的位置。傑克：哇！有好多不同種類的麵包，讓我好難選擇。艾琳：這個長長的是什麼呢？傑克：這是香蒜麵包，它裡面有蒜頭和奶油。你可以把它放在烤箱加熱，然後再將它切片。它通常搭配牛排、義大利麵或凱薩沙拉一起吃。艾琳：我想我之前有吃過這個，但是我真的不是很喜歡蒜頭。那麼這些看起來像牛角的東西是什麼呢？傑克：它們叫做可頌，當早餐是很普遍的。通常我們會把可頌加熱，然後在上面放一些奶油。有時候，人們甚至將它們切半做三明治。鮪魚沙拉和蟹肉沙拉做的可頌都是很美味，而且是很棒的清淡午餐。艾琳：你看這裡，你甚至可以在這裡買到漢堡和熱狗麵包。在台灣他們相當很難買到。也許我可以買一打熱狗麵包，然後，這個週末我們可以在海邊烤燻肉香腸。傑克：這點子聽起來很棒！傑克從架子上拿了兩條土司艾琳：你就只買這些嗎？傑克：是的。一條白土司和一條全麥土司應該可以讓我度過一個禮拜。艾琳：好。我們可以付錢回家了。傑克：進一步考慮後，我想既然都來了，也許我應該買半打甜甜圈。艾琳：我知道你是無法抗拒甜點的。傑克：你太了解我了。
answers: He needed to buy some bread to make sandwiches for his lunch the next day.
She was surprised because they are quite hard to find in Taiwan.
No, she wasn’t because she knew that Jack couldn’t resist buying something sweet.

1.title: Chatting about Facebook
paras: Do you know what tomorrow is?
Yes, it’s your birthday.
Wow! You got it on your first guess. How did you know that?
I saw it on Facebook.
Really?
Yes, it sent me an announcement to remind me of your birthday.
I didn’t know it could do that?
It can do a lot of things.
I know you can play on-line games there and search for friends, but what else can you do?
You can join discussion or interest groups.
What kind of groups?
There is a group for almost everything imaginable. I even saw a group for people who like bacon.
That seems kind of stupid. Are there any useful groups?
Yes, I just joined the Up with People group. Now I can stay connected with some new friends that I met this fall.
I really like exploring new areas in Taiwan. Do you think there is a group for that?
Probably. You can search the groups using key words. So if you type in “explore Taiwan” or “travel Taiwan” you will more than likely get a lot of hits.
Assuming I joined a group like this, what can I expect?

There will be a lot of forums on related topics. Maybe people would share their stories about traveling Taiwan. There might be some recommended places to see. Also, you can expect to see a lot of pictures.
That sounds pretty amazing. Can I upload pictures of my own?
Sure, you can even upload short video clips.
Are book clubs very popular?
Yeah, as are groups for political and social discussions. You can join a group just to show support for a cause too.

dialogue.line: That seems kind of stupid. Are there any useful groups?

dialogue.line: Yes, I just joined the Up with People group. Now I can stay connected with some new friends that I met this fall.

dialogue.speaker: Frieda
dialogue.line: I really like exploring new areas in Taiwan. Do you think there is a group for that?

dialogue.speaker: Tony
dialogue.line: Probably. You can search the groups using key words. So if you type in “explore Taiwan” or “travel Taiwan” you will more than likely get a lot of hits.

dialogue.speaker: Frieda
dialogue.line: Assuming I joined a group like this, what can I expect?

dialogue.speaker: Tony
dialogue.line: There will be a lot of forums on related topics. Maybe people would share their stories about traveling Taiwan. There might be some recommended places to see. Also, you can expect to see a lot of pictures.

dialogue.speaker: Frieda

dialogue.speaker: Tony

1.translation: 一個名叫芙芮達的年輕女生和她的朋友湯尼正在學校的走廊說話，現在是休息時間，他們在等下節課開始。 芙芮達：你知道明天是什麼日子嗎 ？ 湯尼：知道啊，是你的生日啊！ 芙芮達：哇！你第一次就猜中， 你怎麼知道的 ？ 湯尼：我在臉書上看到的。 芙芮達：真的嗎？ 湯尼：它寄給我一個通知，提醒我你的生日。 芙芮達：我不知道臉書有這項功能。 湯尼：在臉書可以做很多事。 芙芮達：我知道你可以玩線上遊戲和搜尋朋友， 但是還能做什麼呢？ 湯尼：你可以加入討論或有興趣的群組。 芙芮達：什麼種類的群組？ 湯尼：只要你想的到的都有， 我甚至看過喜歡培根的群組。 芙芮達：那好像有點蠢，有其他好的群組嗎？ 湯尼：有啊，我剛加入Up with People群組，現在我可以和今年秋天認識的新朋友保持聯繫。 芙芮達：我真的很喜歡探索台灣的新地區，你覺得有這樣的群組嗎？ 湯尼：有可能，你可以用關鍵字搜尋這樣的團體。如果你打「探索台灣」 或 「旅遊台灣」，你很有可能找到很多的相關資料的連結。 芙芮達：假設我加入像這樣的群組，我能預期什麼 ？ 湯尼：會有很多相關主題的論壇，也許人們會分享旅遊台灣的故事，可能也有一些別人推薦的地方可看，而且你可以預期看到很多照片。 芙芮達：這聽起來太棒了，我可以上傳自己的照片嗎？ 湯尼：當然，你甚至可以上傳影片。 芙芮達：讀書俱樂部很普遍嗎？ 湯尼：是啊，與政治和社會議題的討論一樣普遍，你也可以單純加入一個團體表達對某個理念的支持。 芙芮達：我等不及要趕快去上臉書了，我倒想看看到底那裡有什麼群組。 湯尼：也許我可以過去教你如何搜尋群組。 芙芮達： 很好。 嗯，我得去上課了。 湯尼：好，放學後再說。
answers: Facebook sent him an announcement to remind him.
No, she thinks it is stupid.
She can search the groups using key words.

1.title: Computer Troubles
paras: Hi, Nancy.
Hello, cutie. What’s new?
Not too much. How about you?
Oh, I just bought a new computer game called World of Warcraft.
That’s cool. I’ve always wanted to play that game, but my parents won’t let me.
That’s too bad.
Yeah, but maybe I can sneak over to your house and play it.
Sure, but there’s one problem. I can’t get the game to work.
Did you try to re-install it?
Yes, several times.
Do you have enough memory on your computer?
Of course, I have almost 250 free gigabytes.
Are all your drivers up-to-date?
I think so. I just updated my computer last week.
Then maybe your computer is just too old to run the game.
Oh no! Does that mean I have to buy a new computer?
You might get away with just buying a new graphics card.
I hope you’re right. I can’t afford to buy an entire new system, but I do have enough money for a new graphics card.
Good. Why don’t we go buy one right now? Then I can help you install it.
Ok. Let’s go.

1.title: Hiking in the Mountains
paras: Wow. It sure is beautiful here.
Yes, and it is nice and cool up here, too. George hears something.
Did you hear that?
Hear what?
It sounds like bird? They both look up at the tree, but they don’t see any birds there. Then the sound starts again. It’s a high-pitched buzzing noise.
Do you hear it now?
Yes, but it isn’t a bird.
What do you think it is?
It sounds like a cicada to me.

Yeah. That’s probably it and that is why we didn’t see it. They usually stop chirping when something is near. They continue hiking up the winding path towards the top of the mountain. Then George sees something moving in the undergrowth.
Don’t move! I think I saw a snake. Helen stops deadin her tracks.
Where did you see it?
Over there, by that rotting log. As they watch the spot where George supposedly saw the snake, a tiny creature crawls on top of the log to bask in the sunlight. Helen laughs.
That’s not a snake. It’s a grass lizard.

Well I only saw the end of its tail and it looked like a snake to me. They decide to sit down on a big rock next to the path and watch the little lizard bathe in the sun. All of a suddenthey hear a shriek and the cute little lizard disappears.
What in the heck was that? It happened so fast that I didn’t get a good look.
That was a Common Kestrel. It is a small falcon that likes to feed on all kinds of little creatures.
Really?
They even eat venomous snakes, if you can believe it.
They sound pretty neat. How about we go to the library and see if we can find more information on falcons?
Sounds good to me. I’ll race you back to the bottom. Before George even finishes his sentence, Helen is off like a shot.
Wait for me!!!

vocab.zh: (小鳥)發啁啾聲；(昆蟲)發唧唧聲

1.translation: 一位名叫海倫的年輕女子，和她的朋友喬治正在山的一面健行。 海倫：哇！這裡的風景真美啊！ 喬治：是啊，而且山上涼爽宜人。 喬治聽到一些聲音。 喬治： 妳有聽到嗎？ 海倫： 聽到什麼？ 喬治：聽起來好像是鳥叫聲？ 他們同時往樹上看，但沒有看到小鳥，然後聲音又再次出現，那是音調很高的唧唧聲。 喬治：妳現在聽到了嗎？ 海倫：有啊，但那不是鳥叫聲。 喬治：那妳覺得會是什麼？ 海倫：聽起來像是蟬的叫聲。 喬治：對，有可能是蟬，這就是為什麼我們看不見牠的原因。牠們通常在有東西靠近時，就會停止唧唧的聲音。 他們繼續沿著蜿蜒的小徑往山頂走，接著喬治在矮樹叢裡看到有東西在動。 喬治：不要動！我想我看到一條蛇了。 海倫停在原地，一動也不動。 海倫：你在哪裡看到的？ 喬治：那邊，在那腐木的旁邊。 正當他們往喬治可能發現蛇的地方看時，有隻小生物爬到腐木上曬太陽。海倫笑了出來。 海倫：那不是蛇，是一隻草蜥。 喬治：喔，我只看到牠尾巴的那一端，對我來說，那看起來像是蛇。 他們決定在小徑旁的一塊大石頭上坐下來，看著小蜥蜴在做日光浴。突然，他們聽到一陣尖銳的叫聲，那可愛的小蜥蜴就不見了。 海倫：那到底是什麼？發生的太快了，我沒能好好看清楚喬治：那是一隻紅隼，這是一種喜歡捕食各類小動物的小型老鷹。 海倫：真的嗎？ 喬治：你相信嗎? 牠們甚至會吃毒蛇。 海倫：聽起來真是太妙了。我們到圖書館看看是否可以找到更多有關老鷹的資料如何？ 喬治：聽起來不錯。我跟妳比賽看誰先跑到山下。 在喬治還沒說完話時，海倫就像子彈一樣跑開了。 喬治：等等我！
answers: They heard a cicada making a high-pitched buzzing noise.
She stopped dead in her tracks.
It is a small falcon.

1.title: Laughing about Louie
paras: Hey Joe! What do you think old Louie is going to wear today?
Probably, the red blazer and yellow tie he wore yesterday.
The same clothes for two days in a row? No way, man!
Do you want to make a bet?
Sure! My money is on the green tweed jacket and the paisley tie.
Ok. You’re on.
What do you want to wager?
How about, if I win, you have to do my homework for a week?
And what if I win? I don’t need YOU to do MY homework. I don’t want to fail my class!
Ok. How about if you win, I’ll buy you dinner at MOS Burger.
Make it two dinners and you’ve got yourself a deal. The two shake hands just as Mr. Johnson walks into the classroom. Joe jumps into the air cheering.
YES! Red blazer, yellow tie! I win. You can pay up by finishing last night’s homework.
Great.

1.translation: 上課前，兩個學生正坐在教室裡。他們正在談論他們的老師。 丹：嘿，喬！你猜老路易今天會穿什麼呢？ 喬：可能是他昨天穿的紅色西裝外套加黃色領帶。 丹：連續兩天穿同樣的衣服？嘿，不可能吧！ 喬：你想打賭嗎？ 丹：好呀！我賭綠色花呢西裝和佩斯利渦漩紋花呢的領帶。 喬：好，我賭了！ 丹：你想賭什麼？ 喬：如果我贏了，你就得幫我做一星期的回家功課，可以嗎？ 丹：假如我贏了呢？我不要你幫我做功課，我可不想被當掉！ 喬：好哇！如果你贏了，我將請你到摩斯漢堡吃一頓晚餐，可以嗎？ 丹：兩頓晚餐，我就願意賭。 當兩人握手成交時，傑森老師剛好進入教室。喬高興的跳起來歡呼！ 喬：我猜對了！紅色西裝外套加黃色領帶。我贏了！你可以幫我寫昨晚的作業來償還。 丹：很好！
answers: He thinks Louie will wear a red blazer and yellow tie.
They make a bet.
He wagers to do Joe’s homework if he loses.

1.title: The Crush
paras: So, I heard you have a crush on Frieda.
Really? Where did you hear that?
Angel told me all about it at school yesterday.
How would she know?
Because Alice overheard you telling Tony that she was hot.
Well Alice doesn’t know what she is talking about. What other rumors is she saying about me?
She also said that you want to ask Frieda to the dance.
WHAT!? I didn’t say anything about asking her to the dance. She gossips too much. Doesn’t she have anything better to do than spread lies about me?
I guess not. You know what girls are like.
She should just mind her own business.
Maybe, but is there any truth to the rumors. Benson (stuttering): Uh… Of course there isn’t.
The stutter in your voice seems to say otherwise.
All I said was that she was cute. That’s it!
You didn’t say anything about asking her to the dance?
Umm. Uh!
So it is true then. You do have a crush on her. Benson (blushing): Alright, I give up. I’m secretly in love with Frieda.
Why didn’t you just say so in the first place?
Because I don’t want anybody to know.
Well, the whole school already knows. Even Frieda knows.
I feel so embarrassed. I am going to die now. Just put me out of my misery.
If it makes you feel any better, I think I have a crush on Joy.
It does make me feel better knowing that I am not the only one with a crush.
We all go through it at some time. My dad says that it is a part of growing up.
Thanks for sharing that.
No problem. Anything to help a friend.

dialogue.line: Because I don’t want anybody to know.

dialogue.speaker: Leo
dialogue.line: Well, the whole school already knows. Even Frieda knows.

dialogue.speaker: Benson
dialogue.line: I feel so embarrassed. I am going to die now. Just put me out of my misery.

dialogue.speaker: Leo
dialogue.line: If it makes you feel any better, I think I have a crush on Joy.

dialogue.speaker: Benson
dialogue.line: It does make me feel better knowing that I am not the only one with a crush.

dialogue.speaker: Leo
dialogue.line: We all go through it at some time. My dad says that it is a part of growing up.

dialogue.speaker: Benson
dialogue.line: Thanks for sharing that.

dialogue.speaker: Leo
dialogue.line: No problem. Anything to help a friend.

questions: What rumors are going around school about Benson?
Why does Benson try to hide his feelings about Frieda?
What makes Benson feel better?
1.translation: 里歐：我聽說你迷戀上了芙芮達！ 班森：真的嗎？你從哪裡聽說的？ 里歐：昨天在學校時，安琪把這件事全跟我說了。 班森：她是怎麼知道的？ 里歐：因為愛莉絲在無意中聽到你跟東尼說她很辣。 班森：嗯，愛莉絲不知道自己在說什麼吧！她還說了什麼關於我的傳聞嗎？ 里歐：她還說你想要邀請芙芮達去跳舞。 班森：什麼！？我沒說過關於要邀請她去跳舞的事情啊，她太八卦了。難道她沒有比散播我的謠言更好的事要做嗎？ 里歐：我想沒有。你知道的，女生就是那樣。 班森：她應該管好自己的事情就好。 里歐：也許是吧。但是這傳聞有沒有真實性呢？ 班森：(結結巴巴的說):噢......當然沒有。 里歐：你結巴的聲音好像說明事實不是這樣喔！ 班森：我只是說她很可愛，就是這樣而已。 里歐：你沒有說要邀請她去跳舞的事嗎？ 班森：嗯......噢！ 里歐：那麼，這是真的了。你真的迷戀上她了。 班森：(臉紅)好吧，我投降了！我在暗戀芙芮達。 里歐：你為什麼不在一開始就說呢? 班森：因為我不想讓任何人知道。 里歐：好吧，現在全校都知道了，甚至連芙芮達都知道了。 班森：我覺得好尷尬啊，我現在好想死，就讓我遠離我的痛苦吧！ 里歐：我想我迷戀上了喬伊，如果這會讓你覺得好過一點。 班森：這的確讓我覺得好多了，因為我知道不是只有我暗戀別人。 里歐：在某些階段我們都會經歷這樣的事，我爸爸說那是成長過程中的一部分。 班森：感謝你分享這些。 里歐：不客氣，任何可以幫朋友的事情儘管吩咐。
answers: The rumors are that he has a crush on Frieda and that he is going to ask her out.
He tries to hide his feelings because he doesn’t want anybody to know.
When Leo tells him that he has a crush on Joy.

2.title: Conserving Energy
paras: Mom, I want our home to be greener.  Do you know any ways that we can conserve energy?
It’s funny you should mention that. I was just thinking about how we could cut down on power to save money.
So what are we going to do?
The first thing we can do is to buy some energy efficient light bulbs.
That’s a good start. We should also remember to turn off the lights when no one is in the room.
Yes, that will also cut down on power. I am also going to buy a special film for the windows. It reflects sunlight, kind of like a mirror, and helps to keep the house cool.
That way we won’t have to run the air-conditioner so much, right?
Exactly! Air-conditioners use a lot of power. You can also make them run more efficiently by cleaning the filters and other parts that gather dust that restricts airflow.
I’ve heard that TV’s use power even when they aren’t turned on. Is that true?
Yes, unfortunately, as do many other electrical things, like computer monitors, DVD players and stereos.
Is there anything we can do about it?
Yes, we can plug these sorts of things into a power bar. Then when we are finished using them, we simply switch off the power bar and no more power will be wasted.
You sure know a lot about energy conservation, don’t you?
I wouldn’t say that, but I am trying to learn as much as I can.
If everyone thought like you, we could save the world in no time.
I don’t know about that, but I do believe everyone should be more responsible.
You can say that again!

2.translation: 湯姆：媽，我想要讓我們家更環保一點，你知道有什麼方法可以節省能源嗎？ 媽媽：真是有趣，你竟提出這樣的想法，我剛剛才在想我們如何減少用電量以節省開銷。 湯姆：那麼我們要怎麼做呢？ 媽媽：首先，我們可以做的就是買一些省電燈泡。 湯姆：那是個好的開始，我們也應該記得沒有人在房間時要把燈關掉。 媽媽：是的，那也將減少用電量。我也打算去買特殊的玻璃窗貼膜，它有點像鏡子一樣會反射陽光，有助於保持屋內涼爽。 湯姆：那樣子我們冷氣就不用開那麼久了，對嗎？ 媽媽：沒錯！冷氣很耗電。你也可以清洗濾網和其他會聚集灰塵阻塞氣流的地方，使冷氣的運轉更有效率。 湯姆：我聽說電視即使沒有打開時仍然會耗電，這是真的嗎？ 媽媽：是真的，而且不幸地是，很多其他電器用品也是一樣，例如：電腦螢幕、DVD播放器和音響。 湯姆：有什麼我們可以做的？ 媽媽：有的，我們可以把這些電器的插頭插在延長線上，那麼當我們不用這些電器時，只要關掉延長線上的開關，就不會浪費任何能源了。 湯姆：你的確知道很多節省能源的方法，不是嗎？ 媽媽：我不敢這麼說，但我試著盡我所能地學習節省能源的方法。 湯姆：如果每個人都能夠像你這麼想的話，我們很快就可以拯救這個世界了。 媽媽：這我就不知道了，但我相信每個人都應該更有責任感一點。 湯姆：我完全同意！
answers: He wants to learn this because he wants his family’s home to be greener.
It reflects the sunlight and helps keep the house cool.
Yes, it still uses power even when you turn it off.

2.title: In for a Long Semester!
paras: What is your new history teacher like?
Oh my god! He is so strict.
Really? How so?
He has this long list of class rules that everyone has to follow.
Like what?
You aren’t allowed to chew gum, you have to sit straight in your desk, you can’t eat in class and on and on.
What if you break the rules?

It depends on the rule. Like if you are late for class then you have to stay late after school. If you behave badly, you get detention at lunch. Anything worse than that and he will call your parents.
He sounds like a real stickler for rules.
He is! One kid was being disrespectful toward him and almost got expelled from school because of it.
Whoa, I sure wouldn’t want to upset him. I would just follow the rules and keep my mouth shut.
And what about your new chemistry teacher?
I don’t even want to talk about it. He is so mean that it is unbelievable.
What did he do?
One day, some of the boys were fooling around in class and he lost his temper. He actually grabbed one of the boys and dragged him out of the classroom by his hair.
That’s insane! He must have some anger problems or something.
You can say that again. He can’t control his temper at all.
Well. It looks like we are both in for a long semester.
On the bright side of things, the new principal seems very nice.
Yes, he is very generous, too. Today I was short 2 dollars to buy a pop from the machine and he just gave it to me. I didn’t even ask him for it.
That was very thoughtful of him.
It was. I know you wouldn’t have lent me the money.
What are you talking about? Of course I would have.
No, you wouldn’t. You are so cheap. You wouldn’t loan your grandmother 5 dollars to save her life.
You are such a comedian!

2.translation: 詹姆士：你們新來的歷史老師人怎麼樣啊？ 凱瑞：喔，我的天啊！他很嚴格。 詹姆士：真的？怎麼說？ 凱瑞：他列了一長串每個人都必須遵守的班規。 詹姆士：譬如呢？ 凱瑞：你不能嚼口香糖、你必須在座位上筆直的坐好、你上課不可以吃東西等等。 詹姆士：萬一你違反規定呢？ 凱瑞：那要看是什麼規定。如果你上課遲到，放學後你要留到很晚。如果你的表現很差，午餐過後不能出去玩。如果還有比這更嚴重的事，他會打電話給你的父母。 詹姆士：聽起來他是個對規定非常堅持的人。 凱瑞：沒錯！曾有一個小孩對他很不禮貌，因為這件事他差點被退學。 詹姆士：嗚哇！我絕對不會想要惹他生氣。我會乖乖遵守規定，並且不要亂講話。 凱瑞：那你們新的理化老師呢？ 詹姆士：我根本不想談論這件事，他的脾氣暴躁到令人難以置信。 凱瑞：他做了些什麼? 詹姆士：有一天，有一些男生在教室內玩鬧他就動怒了。他竟然一把抓起其中一個男生的頭髮，並且將他拖出教室。 凱瑞：這太瘋狂了！他一定有情緒失控之類的問題。 詹姆士：我同意你的說法，他完全無法控制自己的脾氣。 凱瑞：嗯，我們兩個似乎這學期都一樣難熬了。 詹姆士：往好的方面想，新的校長人似乎不錯。 凱瑞：是啊，他也很大方。今天我在販賣機買汽水時少了兩元，他就給我兩元。我根本沒有跟他要啊。 詹姆士：他真貼心。 凱瑞：的確。我知道如果是你就不會借錢給我。 詹姆士：你說什麼啊？我當然會借給你啊！ 凱瑞：不，你才不會呢。你這麼小氣，連借你奶奶五塊錢來救她的命你也不願意的。 詹姆士：你真愛搞笑！
answers: He thinks so because his teacher has a long list of rules that everyone has to follow.
If he behaved badly, he would get detention.
He lost his temper because some of the boys were fooling around in class.

2.title: Up All Night
paras: What’s up? You look awful.
I feel awful, too.
You have bags under your eyes.
I know I do. It’s because I didn’t sleep at all last night.
Why didn’t you sleep?
I was up all night worrying about today’s test.
I was worried, too, but I didn’t have any trouble sleeping. I slept like a log.
How can you sleep when you are worried about something?
I don’t know. I just close my eyes and fall asleep.
I am so jealous. I wish that I could do that.
Your problem is that you think too much.
I know, but what can I do about it?
Just stop thinking and relax.
That’s easy for you to say. I have too many thoughts going around in my head.
Just take a deep breath and focus on something you find relaxing. What do you find relaxing?
Lying on the beach is relaxing.
Sure, that’s a start. But really try to picture it in your head. Jeffery sits back on the sofa trying to make himself more comfortable and then closes his eyes.
I am lying on a beach towel in the warm sand. There is a big palm tree shading me from the sun. Fluffy white clouds float by on the breeze. Small waves gently crash into the shoreline.
That does sound soothing. Please go on.
Wait! There is a big turtle swimming toward me with something in its mouth.
What is it? What does it have in its mouth?
You won’t believe it.
Tell me! Tell me!
It’s my test. Jeffery opens his eyes and sits up. He looks hopelessly disappointed.
Oh jeez! I guess there is no hope for you.
I guess not. Maybe I need a shrink.
I’ll go get the phonebook.

2.translation: 傑弗瑞在放學之後到咪咪的家中閒聊，他們坐在客廳的沙發上。咪咪：怎麼了，你看起來很不舒服？傑弗瑞：我也感到很不舒服。咪咪：你有黑眼圈。傑弗瑞：我知道，那是因為我昨天一整晚都沒睡。咪咪：為什麼你沒有睡？傑弗瑞：我昨晚整晚沒睡，都在擔心今天的考試。咪咪：我也很擔心啊！但是我沒有睡不著的問題，我睡得很沉。傑弗瑞：你在擔心某件事的時候怎麼還能睡得著？咪咪：我不知道，我只是閉上眼睛就睡著了。傑弗瑞：我好羨慕你喔，我希望我也可以這樣。咪咪：你的問題就是你想太多了。傑弗瑞：我知道，但我該怎麼做呢？咪咪：不要去想事情，然後放鬆。傑弗瑞：你說得倒容易，我腦中有太多的想法在進行。咪咪：先來個深呼吸，然後專注於某個可以讓你放鬆的事情。什麼事可以讓你放鬆？傑弗瑞：躺在海灘上可以讓我放鬆。咪咪：是，這是個開始，試著在你腦海中呈現那個畫面。傑弗瑞坐回沙發，試圖讓自己更舒適一點，然後閉上眼睛。傑弗瑞：在溫暖的沙灘上，我躺在一條海灘浴巾上面，還有一棵很大的棕櫚樹幫我遮住陽光。輕軟的白雲隨微風飄過，小浪輕輕地拍打著海岸。咪咪：那聽起來很舒服，請繼續吧！傑弗瑞：等等，有一隻烏龜嘴巴咬著東西的朝我游過來。咪咪：是什麼？是什麼東西在他嘴巴裡？傑弗瑞：你不會相信的。咪咪：告訴我！告訴我！傑弗瑞：是我的考卷。傑弗瑞睜開眼睛，並且坐起來。他看起來很失望，一副無可救藥的樣子。咪咪：喔，天啊！我想你是沒有希望了。傑弗瑞：我想也是如此。也許我需要一位心理醫生。咪咪：我去拿電話簿。
answers: He is so tired because he was up all night worrying about the next day’s test.
She tries to teach him how to relax.
He finds lying on the beach relaxing.

2.title: Waiting for the Washroom
paras: Could you hurry up in there? I’m going to be late for school.
Hold your horses! I’ll be out in a minute. 5 minutes later, Jeff’s patience is wearing thin.
Come on! Did you die in there or what?
I said that I’ll be out in a minute.
Yeah, and that was like 10 minutes ago.
Okay. Okay. One second. Another 5 minutes pass. Jeff has to pee so badly that he is jumping up and down nervously and banging on the door.
God, Michelle! This isn’t funny. I’m going to pee myself. Get a move on.
Almost finished.

That’s it! If you don’t open the door this instant, I am going to tell mom that you are dating Billy Fischer. The door flies open. Michelle has a very accommodating and innocent looking smile on her face.
It’s about time.
You won’t tell mom about...you know?
Not if you stop hogging the bathroom every morning.
Okay then. It’s a deal.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Could you hurry up in there? I’m going to be late for school.

dialogue.speaker: Michelle
dialogue.line: Hold your horses! I’ll be out in a minute. 5 minutes later, Jeff’s patience is wearing thin.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Come on! Did you die in there or what?

dialogue.speaker: Michelle
dialogue.line: I said that I’ll be out in a minute.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Yeah, and that was like 10 minutes ago.

dialogue.speaker: Michelle
dialogue.line: Okay. Okay. One second. Another 5 minutes pass. Jeff has to pee so badly that he is jumping up and down nervously and banging on the door.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: God, Michelle! This isn’t funny. I’m going to pee myself. Get a move on.

dialogue.speaker: Michelle
dialogue.line: Almost finished.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: That’s it! If you don’t open the door this instant, I am going to tell mom that you are dating Billy Fischer. The door flies open. Michelle has a very accommodating and innocent looking smile on her face.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: It’s about time.

dialogue.speaker: Michelle
dialogue.line: You won’t tell mom about...you know?

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Not if you stop hogging the bathroom every morning.

dialogue.speaker: Michelle
dialogue.line: Okay then. It’s a deal.

questions: Why does Jeff want his sister to hurry up?
Why is Jeff’s patience wearing thin?
What does Jeff finally do to make her come up?
2.translation: 現在是星期一的早上八點，傑夫正在等他的姐姐蜜雪兒走出浴室。 傑夫：你可以快一點嗎？我上學快遲到了。 蜜雪兒：不要急，我一分鐘後就出來了。 五分鐘後，傑夫的耐性逐漸消失。 傑夫：趕快啦！你是死在浴室還是怎麼了？ 蜜雪兒：我說過了，再一分鐘就出去了。 傑夫：是啊，那好像是十分鐘前說的話。 蜜雪兒：好好，再一下下。 又過了五分鐘，傑夫尿急到焦急地跳上跳下，並且一直用力敲門。 傑夫：天啊！蜜雪兒。這一點都不好笑，我快尿褲子了，趕快！ 蜜雪兒：快好了。 傑夫：夠了！如果你不馬上開門的話，我要去告訴媽媽你和比利‧費爾交往。 門開了。蜜雪兒臉上露出親切純真的微笑。 傑夫：該是時候了。 蜜雪兒：你不會告訴媽媽有關於……你知道的嘛! 傑夫：只要你不會每天早上都一直佔用浴室的話。 蜜雪兒：好，就這麼說定。
answers: He wants her to hurry up because he is afraid he’ll be late for school.
It is wearing thin because his sister tells him she’ll be out in a minute several times, but she doesn’t come out.
He threatens to tell their mother that Michelle is dating Billy Fischer.

2.title: What to Wear?
paras: What are you going to wear to the school dance tomorrow?
I am going to wear the same black dress that I wore to your birthday party.
Yeah, that’s a good idea. You look very elegant in that dress. It suits you.
Thanks for the compliment. And what about you? What are you going to wear?
I have absolutely no idea! Do you have any suggestions?
How about the red skirt and yellow blouse you wore on your first date with Jackson?
No, that outfit brings back bad memories, not to mention that it is a little too small now.
Right. I forgot about him. I won’t mention it again.
Thanks. Do you have any other suggestions?
Well, let me think… Do you still have that dark gray pantsuit you wore to your brother’s graduation?
Don’t you think it looks too formal for a dance?
I suppose you are right. Something a little more casual would be better. Why don’t we just go shopping so that you can buy a new outfit?
Sure. We can go to Guan Ming Street in Yuanlin. There are so many clothing shops and boutiques there that I’m sure to find something to wear.
OK. Let’s go.

2.translation: 愛麗絲：明天學校的舞會妳要穿什麼? 梅姬：我打算要穿在妳的生日派對上穿的那一件黑色洋裝。 愛麗絲：那是一個好主意。妳穿那一件禮服看起來很優美高雅，很適合妳。 梅姬：謝謝妳的讚美，那妳呢？妳要穿什麼? 愛麗絲：我一點概念都沒有，妳有任何的建議嗎？ 梅姬：妳和傑克森第一次約會穿的那件紅色裙子和黃色襯衫如何？ 愛麗絲：不好，那套衣服會帶給我不好的回憶，更別提那套衣服現在也有點太小了。 梅姬：對喔，我把他的事給忘了，我不會再提到他了。 愛麗絲：多謝了，妳還有其他的建議嗎？ 梅姬：嗯，讓我想想……妳在妳哥哥的畢業典禮上穿的那套深灰色套裝還在嗎？ 愛麗絲：妳不覺得對舞會來說，那件看起來太正式了嗎？ 梅姬：我想妳說的沒錯，休閒一點的衣服會好一點。我們何不現在去逛街，讓妳添購一套新衣服？ 愛麗絲：好啊，我們可以去員林的光明街，那裡有很多服飾店和時裝精品店，我相信一定可以找到適合的衣服穿。 梅姬：好的，我們走吧。
answers: She doesn’t want to wear it because it brings back bad memories and it is too small.
Her second suggestion is that Alice wear the dark gray pantsuit that she wore to her brother’s graduation.
They decide to go shopping so that Alice can buy a new outfit.

2.title: Unemployed
paras: Hey Chuck. Long time no see.
Yeah, it’s been a while.
So what have you been up to?
Not much, I’m currently unemployed.
Sorry to hear that. Any luck finding a new job?
Not yet, but I have a few leads.
Did you check the classifieds?
Yes, but nothing looked too appealing.
Well, beggars can’t be choosers.
I know, I know. And the bills aren’t going to pay themselves either, right?
Exactly. Why don’t you stop by my office tomorrow? I think there is an opening.
Great! I’ll do that. See you tomorrow then.

2.translation: 兩個老朋友在7-11不期而遇。 賴瑞：嘿！查克，好久不見了。 查克：是啊，已經有一段時間了。 賴瑞：你最近在忙什麼呢？ 查克：沒什麼，我目前失業中。 賴瑞：聽到這個消息真為你感到難過。新工作找的還順利嗎？ 查克：還沒找到，但是我有一些資訊。 賴瑞：你查過分類廣告了嗎？ 查克：有啊，但是沒有令我很感興趣的工作。 賴瑞：嗯，乞丐沒有選擇的餘地。 查克：我知道，而且帳單也不會自動就付清，不是嗎？ 賴瑞：沒錯！不然你明天到我辦公室來，我想我們有個空缺。 查克：太好了！我一定會去的，明天見。
answers: He has been looking for a new job.
He looked in the classified section of the newspaper.
He tells him this because he thinks there might be a job opening.

2.title: Arguing about Chores
paras: It’s time to clean up. Sally, you clear the table and Jesse, you can do the dishes.
Me, do the dishes? But it’s not my turn. I did them yesterday.
Don’t complain. I had to do them all weekend while you were out playing baseball.
It’s not my fault that I had a tournament to go to.
Stop arguing and just do them.
But Thursday is her day to wash the dishes, not mine. Why do I always get stuck doing them?
You are such a baby. You don’t “always” do the dishes. I do them more than you.
How about the time I had to do them five weeks in a row and you didn’t even help.
Duh! My arm was in a cast. Remember?
So what? You’re just making excuses. I still had to do them when my ankle was sprained.
And that’s the same? Think about it.
You could’ve been half finished by now if you weren’t sitting around complaining.

She never has to do anything. I have to do everything. I hate you. Jesse storms off to his room. Mrs. Chen (Yelling up the stairs): The dishes will still be waiting for you in the sink when you’re done pouting.
Is it too late to give him up for adoption?

2.translation: 陳家人剛吃完晚餐。 陳太太：該收拾了！莎莉，妳清理桌子。傑西，你去洗碗。 傑西：我洗碗？又不是輪到我！我昨天才洗過而已。 莎莉：不要再發牢騷了，當你週末去打棒球時，都是我洗的。 傑西：我去參加棒球比賽又不是我的錯。 陳太太：不要再爭論了，去洗就對了！ 傑西：但是星期四是該她洗碗，不是我。為什麼總是變成我在做？ 莎莉：真是孩子氣！你才沒有「老是」在洗碗呢！我洗的比你多。 傑西：那麼有次我必須連續洗五個星期的碗，而妳卻是一點忙也沒幫。 莎莉：拜託！那時我的手臂上了石膏，你不記得嗎？ 傑西：那又怎麼樣？妳只是在找藉口而已。當我的腳扭傷時，還不是要洗碗。 莎莉：用大腦想一想，情況一樣嗎？ 陳太太：如果你們不是光坐在那兒發牢騷，現在早已完成一半了。 傑西：她什麼事都不用做，而我卻要樣樣做。我討厭妳。 傑西怒氣沖沖回到他的房間。 陳太太(對著樓上大叫)：當你氣消之後，這些碗盤還是留在水槽裡等你去洗。 莎莉：現在要把他送人領養是否太遲了？
answers: They are fighting about who has to do the dishes.
He is upset because he thinks he always gets stuck doing the dishes.
He had to do them because Sally’s arm was broken and in a cast.

2.title: What to do for the Pageant?
paras: So children...what do you want to do for the Christmas pageant this year?
How about nothing?
Sam just doesn’t like to perform in front of people.
Yeah. He gets stage fright.
Really? That surprises me. He certainly likes to act out in front of the class.
No, I don’t.
Sure you do. You are the class clown. You’ll do anything to get a laugh.
We could have him do a comedy act for the Christmas show.
Great idea. You are really funny, Sam.
That sounds like a wonderful plan. You could tell some jokes about Christmas. So what about the rest of you?
You mean we still have to do something, too?
Darn, I thought Sam could represent the whole class.
Nice try!

vocab.zh: 慶祝聖誕節的表演節目

2.translation: 老師：小朋友，今年你們聖誕節要表演什麼節目？ 山姆：可以不表演嗎？ 南西：山姆不喜歡在別人前面表演。 馬姬：是啊！他上台會怯場。 老師：真的嗎？我很驚訝，他真的很喜歡在班上同學面前表演啊！ 山姆：不，我才不喜歡呢！ 老師：你當然喜歡，你是班上的開心果，為了博君一笑你什麼事都願意嘗試。 馬姬：我們可以請他在聖誕節表演一段喜劇。 南西：好主意，山姆你真的很滑稽。 老師：這個計畫聽起來很棒，你可以說說有關聖誕節的笑話。那麼其餘的人要表演什麼？ 南西：你是說我們還是要表演節目？ 馬姬：討厭，我還以為山姆可以代表全班呢！ 老師：你想得美！(註：Nice try直接翻成中文是「不錯的嘗試」，意思指某人想出一個計策試著要讓你上當，但若是他的詭計被你看穿了，你就可以對他說Nice try!)
answers: She thinks he gets stage fright.
She is surprised because he likes to act out in class.
She thinks he should tell some jokes about Christmas.

2.title: Holiday Plans
paras: So...any special plans for the holidays?
Not really. My wife wants to go visit her folks down in Taidong, but other than that, it will just be R ‘n’ R.
That doesn’t sound too bad. My wife has every second planned out for me.
I am sorry to hear that.

Me, too. It wouldn’t be too bad if she would give me a little time to recuperate from work, but OH NO, she has to make sure that every waking moment is spent visiting some long lost cousin or some old senile uncle who doesn’t even remember us. Elisiah (killing himself laughing): Think of all the holiday cheer you’ll spread.
Spreading out on the couch sounds better to me.
Look at the bright side; at least you don’t have to work.
Are you serious? I would rather work, but the boss won’t pay me time and a half for working on a holiday.
That sounds like him. He’s a real scrooge.
Whatever, he’s off my Christmas card list for good.

vocab.zh: Rest and Relaxation 休息和放鬆

2.translation: 今天是聖誕假期前最後一天的工作日，兩個同事在吃午餐的時候聊天。 蓋博：這個假期有沒有特別的計劃啊？ 艾利喜：不算有，我太太要去台東看她的父母，除此之外，就只是休息和放鬆而已。 蓋博：聽起來還不錯嘛！我太太已經把我所有的時間都安排好了。 艾利喜：聽到你這麼說真為你感到難過。 蓋博：我也是啊！如果她能給我一點時間，從工作的疲倦中恢復過來，事情就不會那麼糟。但是她沒有，她一定要把所有清醒的時間都花在拜訪某個失散已久的表姐，或某位老的根本就已經不記得我們的伯父上。 艾利喜：(笑死了)試想你將為大家散播節慶的歡樂氣氛。 蓋博：躺在沙發上面聽起來還好一點呢！(註：spread在此是雙關語，它有「散播」和「伸展」的意思。 艾利喜：往好的那一面看，至少你不用工作。 蓋博：你是說真的嗎？老兄，我還寧願上班呢？但是，老闆卻不會因我假日工作而多付我一點五倍的薪水。 艾利喜：聽起來像是他的作風沒錯，他是個吝嗇鬼。 蓋博：管他的，反正他已經永遠不會列入我寄聖誕卡的名單中了。
answers: He is going to spend most of them getting some rest and relaxation.
He would rather work because his wife has every second planned out for him.
He can’t work because his boss won’t pay him time and a half.

2.title: A Field Trip to the Louvre
paras: I am excited. Jessie’s mother: So, what is it that you couldn’t wait to tell me?
(jumping up and down): My class is going on a FIELD TRIP. Jessie’s mother: And? Classes go on field trip all the time. That doesn’t seem like anything to get worked up over.
But we are going to an exhibit. Jessie’s mother (being sarcastic): What a surprise; a field trip to an exhibit.
In a museum. Jessie’s mother (with even more sarcasm): How shocking! You must be the first class in history to go to a museum.
In France. Jessie’s mother: What a revelation! (long pause) Say that again.
We are going to a museum. Jessie’s mother: Yes, yes, I know that, but where exactly?
In France. Jessie’s mother (in disbelief): You are going to the Louvre?
Yes, our class won a contest and the government is paying for our entire class to go to Paris. Jessie’s mother: Thank god! I thought your father was going to have to pay.

No, no, no. The government is covering the whole bill. The only thing you have to do is to sign a consent form. Jessie’s mother: I guess if the government is covering the cost then signing my name is the least I can do.
But… Jessie’s mother: There is always a “but.”
You have to give me some spending money. Jessie’s mother (laughing): Ok. Here is 100NT. Have fun in Paris!
Stop joking around. What in the heck am I going to do with 100 dollars in Paris? Jessie’s mother: The same thing you’d do with ten, except you can do it ten times.
You’re so funny. You’re the worst comedian and mother ever. Jessie’s mother: Maybe, but at least I have more than 100 dollars.
(shaking her head and looking less than amused) Don’t expect a souvenir.

dialogue.speaker: Jessie
dialogue.line: I am excited. Jessie’s mother: So, what is it that you couldn’t wait to tell me?

dialogue.speaker: Jessie
dialogue.line: (jumping up and down): My class is going on a FIELD TRIP. Jessie’s mother: And? Classes go on field trip all the time. That doesn’t seem like anything to get worked up over.

dialogue.speaker: Jessie
dialogue.line: But we are going to an exhibit. Jessie’s mother (being sarcastic): What a surprise; a field trip to an exhibit.

dialogue.speaker: Jessie
dialogue.line: In a museum. Jessie’s mother (with even more sarcasm): How shocking! You must be the first class in history to go to a museum.

dialogue.speaker: Jessie
dialogue.line: In France. Jessie’s mother: What a revelation! (long pause) Say that again.

dialogue.speaker: Jessie
dialogue.line: We are going to a museum. Jessie’s mother: Yes, yes, I know that, but where exactly?

dialogue.speaker: Jessie
dialogue.line: In France. Jessie’s mother (in disbelief): You are going to the Louvre?

dialogue.speaker: Jessie
dialogue.line: Yes, our class won a contest and the government is paying for our entire class to go to Paris. Jessie’s mother: Thank god! I thought your father was going to have to pay.

dialogue.speaker: Jessie
dialogue.line: No, no, no. The government is covering the whole bill. The only thing you have to do is to sign a consent form. Jessie’s mother: I guess if the government is covering the cost then signing my name is the least I can do.

dialogue.speaker: Jessie
dialogue.line: But… Jessie’s mother: There is always a “but.”

dialogue.speaker: Jessie
dialogue.line: You have to give me some spending money. Jessie’s mother (laughing): Ok. Here is 100NT. Have fun in Paris!

dialogue.speaker: Jessie
dialogue.line: Stop joking around. What in the heck am I going to do with 100 dollars in Paris? Jessie’s mother: The same thing you’d do with ten, except you can do it ten times.

dialogue.speaker: Jessie
dialogue.line: You’re so funny. You’re the worst comedian and mother ever. Jessie’s mother: Maybe, but at least I have more than 100 dollars.

dialogue.speaker: Jessie
dialogue.line: (shaking her head and looking less than amused) Don’t expect a souvenir.

questions: Why did Jessie run all the way home from school?
Why wasn’t Jessie’s mother surprised that she was going on a field trip to a museum?
Why did Jessie think her mother was the worst mother ever?

vocab.zh: 哎呀(用來表達驚訝的感嘆詞)

2.translation: 在一個星期三的下午，將近五點的時候，潔西飛快的衝進大門，將書包丟在沙發上，並且跑進廚房。 潔西的媽媽：哎呀，妳把我嚇死了！ 潔西(試著喘息)：對不起啦，因為我一路從學校跑回來要告訴妳一件事。 潔西的媽媽：如果妳真的一路跑回來，那妳一定是很興奮，妳不曾跑到任何地方。 潔西：我是很興奮啊。 潔西的媽媽：那麼是什麼事讓妳等不及要告訴我呢？ 潔西(跳上跳下的)：我們班要去戶外教學。 潔西的媽媽：然後呢？班級都會去戶外教學啊，這似乎不是什麼讓人特別興奮的理由。 潔西：但是我們要去看展覽。 潔西的媽媽(用挖苦的口氣)：真是令人驚訝呢！戶外教學去看展覽。 潔西：在一個博物館裡。 潔西的媽媽(用更諷刺的口吻)：多麼令人震驚呀！你們一定是史上第一個去博物館的班級呢! 潔西：在法國。 潔西的媽媽：真是讓人意想不到啊！(停了好一會兒之後)妳……再說一次。 潔西：我們要去一個博物館。 潔西的媽媽：是的，是的，我知道，但是到底要去哪裡？ 潔西：法國。 潔西的媽媽(難以置信地)：難道你們要去羅浮宮？ 潔西：是的，我們班贏得了一個競賽，政府要補助我們全班去巴黎的費用。 潔西的媽媽：真是謝天謝地！我還以為妳爸爸要付這筆費用。 潔西：喔不！政府會負擔所有的費用，妳只需要簽這份同意書。 潔西的媽媽：我想如果政府要負擔所有的費用，那簽名是我能做的最起碼的事。 潔西：可是…… 潔西的媽媽：凡事總是會有個「可是」。 潔西：妳要給我一些零用錢啊。 潔西的媽媽(笑出來)：好的，這裡有一百塊，希望妳在巴黎玩得愉快。 潔西：不要再開玩笑了啦，一百塊到底能在巴黎能做什麼啊？ 潔西的媽媽：跟十元能做的事情一樣，只是你可以做十次。 潔西：妳真的很好笑，妳真是有史以來最差勁的喜劇演員和媽媽了。 潔西的媽媽：也許吧，但是至少我有比一百塊還多的錢。 潔西(搖搖頭，看起來不太高興)：那妳也別想要有紀念品了。
answers: She was excited and wanted to tell her mother the news.
She wasn’t surprised because it is not unusual for a class to go to a museum on a field trip.
She thought this because her mother was only going to give her 100NT in spending money.

3.title: The Androgynous New Student
paras: Hey, you’re new around here, aren’t you?
Yeah, it’s my first week at this school. Tyler is a bit attracted to the feminine beauty of the new student and becomes bold.
Listen, there is a school dance this weekend and it would be a good place to meet new people. And seeing I don’t have a date, well, maybe you’d like to go with me?
(chuckling) I’m sorry. I’m not gay. Tyler suddenly realizes that the new student is not a girl. He is utterly embarrassed.

Uh! Sorry. Wow, talk about sticking your foot in your mouth. Boy is my face red. NS (laughing wildly): You thought I was a girl! I don’t know whether to laugh or cry. They both laugh at the humorous misunderstanding.

By the way, my name is Pat. And yes I realize the irony. Tyler (trying desperately to not to laugh): You have to be kidding. Sorry, but that is too funny. Anyway, it is nice to meet you “Pat”. It is a strange start to a new friendship.

Why does Tyler mistake the new student for a girl?

vocab.zh: 說錯了話；說了不該說的話

3.translation: 泰勒在走廊上散步消磨時間，等待下堂課的到來。他看到一位新同學，決定過去跟他交談。 泰勒：嘿！你是新來的，不是嗎? 新同學：是啊，這是我到這所學校的第一週。 泰勒有點被這位新同學的女性美吸引，人變得大膽起來。 泰勒：我跟你說喔！週末有一場學校舞會，這是認識新朋友的好地方。嗯，由於我沒有舞伴，也許你會想跟我一起去參加舞會呢？ 新同學：（咯咯地笑）很抱歉，我可不是同性戀者喔！ 泰勒突然了解這位新同學並不是女生，他感到非常地尷尬。 泰勒：啊！對不起，真是說錯話了。天啊！我的臉都紅了呢！ 新同學（大聲狂笑）：你以為我是個女孩啊！我真不知道該哭還是該笑呢！ 他們兩個都因為這個可笑的誤會而大笑。 新同學：順便提一下，我叫佩特。是的，我了解這可是很諷刺呢！ 泰勒（努力忍著不笑）：你一定是在開玩笑。不好意思，但那實在太好笑了。無論如何，很高興認識你，「佩特」！ 這一段新友誼的開始還真是奇怪啊！
answers: It is his first week at this school.
He mistakes him for a girl because the new student is looks feminine.
It is ironic because Pat can be a girl’s or a boy’s name.

3.title: Summer’s Over
paras: Oh my god, the summer is almost over.
Yeah, I know. I was just getting used to sitting on my butt doing absolutely nothing and now it’s time to head back to school.
How am I ever going to get up in the morning? I am so used to sleeping in.
Tell me about it! I’ve been sleeping till noon every day.
I guess there won’t be any more late nights playing video games and watching cartoons.
I’m sure there will be plenty of late nights doing homework, though.
You got that right. Argh!
At least we will get to see some of our friends.
I guess that’s true, but at what cost? We will be too busy to talk to them.
Maybe, we should just quit and get a job.
But we are only ten. Who would hire us?
I suppose you’re right. Maybe, when we’re older we can quit.
Sure, like 15. That is really old!
Yeah, those guys have it made.

3.translation: 大衛: 天啊，這個夏天已接近尾聲了！ 法蘭克: 是的，我知道啊！我已經習慣坐著沒事幹，而現在該是回學校上課的時候了。 大衛: 我早上怎麼起得了床啊？我已經習慣睡覺賴床了。 法蘭克: 可不是嗎，我每天都睡到中午。 大衛: 我想再也不能熬夜玩電視遊樂器以及看卡通了。 法蘭克: 不過我確定以後要經常熬夜寫作業。 大衛: 你講的沒錯，唉！ 法蘭克: 至少我們都能夠再見到一些朋友。 大衛: 我想的沒錯，但是代價呢？我們會忙到沒時間和他們說話。 法蘭克: 也許我們該放棄學業，並且找份工作。 大衛: 但是我們才十歲，誰會僱用我們？ 法蘭克: 我想你講的沒錯。也許等我們大一點時再放棄學業。 大衛: 的確，好比說十五歲，那已經很大了！ 法蘭克: 是啊，那些人真好！
answers: He is worried because he is used to sleeping in.
He has been sleeping till noon every day.
He thinks there will be lots of late because they will be doing homework.
He thinks it is a bad idea because nobody would hire them.

3.title: Asking For Assistance
paras: Excuse me.
Yes, can I help you?
I’m sorry to bother you, but I have a question I was hoping you could help me with.
No problem! Fire away.
Where are the PSP games?
They are in the electronics department on the aisle 4 next to the kitchen appliances. Evan leaves briefly, but returns.
I really hate to be a nuisance, but I’d like to see one of the games and it’s locked inside a display case.
No worries! I’ll open it for you. The sales associate leads Evan back to the electronics to open the cabinet.
Thanks a lot. You’ve been a real help.
That’s what I’m here for.

3.translation: 爾文正要購買一台PSP遊戲機，但是他找不到這項產品。(註：PSP是PlayStation Portable的縮寫，它是Sony公司發售的 掌上型遊戲機。) 爾文：請問一下。 店員：是的，我能爲您服務嗎？ 爾文：很抱歉打擾你，我有一個問題希望你能幫忙一下。 店員：沒問題，請說吧！ 爾文：PSP遊戲機放在什麼地方呢？ 店員：放在第四個走道的電子商品區，就在廚房家電用品旁邊。 爾文離開了一下，但是又回來。 爾文：我實在不想那麼煩人，不過我想看看其中一台遊戲機，但是它鎖在展示櫃裡面。 店員：別擔心，我幫您打開。 店員帶著爾文回到電子區把櫃子打開。 爾文：非常感謝你，你真是幫了很多忙。 店員：這是我職責所在啊！
answers: They are in the electronics department.
He can’t take a look at it because it is locked inside a display case.
No, he isn’t. He seems very helpful.

3.title: A Valentine’s Vacation Gone Wrong
paras: Where are we going to have dinner tonight?
How about you pick the restaurant?
What about a Japanese restaurant?
We just had Japanese food last Saturday.
Let’s go to an all-you-can-eat place then.
You know I don’t like buffets.
I know! Korean.
I don’t feel like Korean today. Fred (growing frustrated): Italian?
We can have Italian any time. Fred (angrily): Then how about instant noodles at 7-11.
Why? What’s your problem?

You told me to choose a restaurant, but you are being fussy and shooting down all of my suggestions. Why don’t you just pick it yourself? June (pouting): Then let’s just go back to the hotel and go to bed. Fred (rolling his eyes): Here we go!
What is that supposed to mean?
Nothing. Nothing.
You are ruining my Valentine’s Day.
Me? That’s right. It’s always my fault.
If you would just pick a restaurant that I LIKED…
What am I, a mind reader or something? Why can’t you just tell me?
You don’t love me. Fred (sarcastically): Right. I don’t love you because I can’t read your mind.
If you loved me, you would know what I like.
I know one thing you like.
What’s that?
Arguing.
You are so childish. I hate you! June stomps back to the car as fast as she can. She slams the door. Fred hears the locks on the car door click. He shakes his head and mutters to himself…
It’s going to be a long night.

3.translation: 弗雷德和珍正坐在墾丁海邊。 弗雷德：我們要去哪裡吃晚餐呢？ 珍：你選一家餐廳如何？ 弗雷德：那日式餐廳好嗎？ 珍：我們上星期六才吃過日本料理。 弗雷德：那讓我們去一個吃到飽的餐廳。 珍：你知道我不喜歡自助餐。 弗雷德：我知道了，韓式餐廳。 珍：我今天不想吃韓式料理。 弗雷德 （開始感到洩氣了）：義式的呢？ 珍：我們任何時候都可以吃義式料理。 弗雷德（生氣地）：那麼便利商店的泡麵如何呢？ 珍：為什麼？你是怎麼搞的？ 弗雷德：妳要我選一家餐廳，但是妳很挑剔而且否決我所有的建議。為什麼妳不自己選一家呢？ 珍（嘟著嘴）: 那我們就回去飯店，然後睡覺。 弗雷德（翻白眼）：好，我們走！ 珍：你這是什麼意思？ 弗雷德：沒事，沒事！ 珍：你正毀了我的情人節。 弗雷德：我？沒錯！每次都是我的錯。 珍：如果你能選一間我喜歡的餐廳的話… 弗雷德：我是誰呢？我會讀心術還是有甚麼特殊能力？為什麼妳就不能直接告訴我呢？ 珍：你不愛我。 弗雷德（諷刺地）：是的，因為我無法了解妳的心，所以我不愛你。 珍：如果你愛我，你會知道我喜歡什麼的。 弗雷德：我知道妳喜歡的一件事珍：那是什麼？ 弗雷德：吵架。 珍：你真幼稚，我討厭你。 珍用她最快的速度跺步回到車上，她砰的一聲地用力地把門關上，弗雷德聽到了車鎖喀嚓的聲音，他搖搖頭低聲地對自己說...... 弗雷德：這將會是個漫長的夜晚。
answers: She doesn’t want it because they just had it recently.
He gets angry because June keeps saying no to all of his suggestions and is being fussy.
She concludes that Fred doesn’t love her.

3.title: Something in the Woods
paras: Did you hear that?
Yeah. What do you think it is?
I don’t know. It sounded like a pig.
A pig? It sounded more like a ghost to me.
A pig! A ghost! You are both crazy. I think it is a wolf.
We are in Taiwan. There aren’t any wolves here.
Should we go and check it out?
Ok, but Harriet has to go first.
Why me?
Because you are the fastest.
No, I’m not. I’m the slowest. That’s why you want me to go first.
What’s your point?
Whatever is hiding in the bushes will catch me first and you both can get away safely while it eats me.
It’s like the old saying, “I don’t have to outrun the bear; I just have to outrun you.”

Very funny. You’re a real comedian. Suddenly a little white ball of fur streaks across the campsite and disappears into the bushes on the other side. Sally and Tina scream and then run and hide in the tent. The little furry animal slowly hops back out of the bushes toward Harriet. She begins to laugh.
Aww! It’s just a cute little bunny. It’s so sweet. The other girls come out from hiding to see.
That is what we were worried about. I feel stupid now.

Me, too. This little guy wouldn’t hurt a flea. Before the girls realize what is happening, the cute little bunny lunges at them and tears them to pieces. It was their first and last encounter with the rare were-rabbit.

dialogue.line: Did you hear that?

dialogue.speaker: Tina
dialogue.line: Yeah. What do you think it is?

dialogue.speaker: Harriet
dialogue.line: I don’t know. It sounded like a pig.

dialogue.speaker: Sally
dialogue.line: A pig? It sounded more like a ghost to me.

dialogue.speaker: Tina
dialogue.line: A pig! A ghost! You are both crazy. I think it is a wolf.

dialogue.speaker: Harriet
dialogue.line: We are in Taiwan. There aren’t any wolves here.

dialogue.speaker: Sally
dialogue.line: Should we go and check it out?

dialogue.speaker: Tina
dialogue.line: Ok, but Harriet has to go first.

dialogue.speaker: Harriet
dialogue.line: Why me?

dialogue.speaker: Sally
dialogue.line: Because you are the fastest.

dialogue.speaker: Harriet
dialogue.line: No, I’m not. I’m the slowest. That’s why you want me to go first.

dialogue.speaker: Tina
dialogue.line: What’s your point?

dialogue.speaker: Harriet
dialogue.line: Whatever is hiding in the bushes will catch me first and you both can get away safely while it eats me.

dialogue.speaker: Sally
dialogue.line: It’s like the old saying, “I don’t have to outrun the bear; I just have to outrun you.”

dialogue.speaker: Harriet
dialogue.line: Very funny. You’re a real comedian. Suddenly a little white ball of fur streaks across the campsite and disappears into the bushes on the other side. Sally and Tina scream and then run and hide in the tent. The little furry animal slowly hops back out of the bushes toward Harriet. She begins to laugh.

dialogue.speaker: Harriet
dialogue.line: Aww! It’s just a cute little bunny. It’s so sweet. The other girls come out from hiding to see.

dialogue.speaker: Sally
dialogue.line: That is what we were worried about. I feel stupid now.

dialogue.speaker: Tina
dialogue.line: Me, too. This little guy wouldn’t hurt a flea. Before the girls realize what is happening, the cute little bunny lunges at them and tears them to pieces. It was their first and last encounter with the rare were-rabbit.

questions: Why couldn’t the sound have been made by a wolf?
Why do the other girls want Harriet to check it out?
What do Sally and Tina do when they see the rabbit run across the campsite?

vocab.zh: 兔人(註：來自狼人概念的自創單字)

3.translation: 在一個月圓的晚上，三個女孩正在樹林裡露營。天很黑，當她們聽到樹林裡有東西時，她們正圍著營火坐著。 莎莉：妳有聽到那個聲音嗎？ 蒂娜：有！妳覺得那是什麼呢？ 哈莉特：我不知道，那聽起來像是豬的聲音。 莎莉：豬？我覺得聽起來比較像是鬼的聲音。 蒂娜：豬！鬼！你們兩個都瘋了，我覺得那是狼。 哈莉特：我們在台灣，這裡沒有狼。 莎莉：我們應該要去看看嗎？ 蒂娜：好啊，但是哈莉特要先走。 哈莉特：為什麼是我？ 莎莉：因為妳跑最快。 哈莉特：不，我不是，我是最慢的。這是妳要我走在第一個的原因嗎？ 蒂娜：妳想講的是什麼？ 哈莉特：不管是什麼東西在叢林裡，它會第一個捉到我，然後你們兩個就可以趁著它在吃我的時候安全地逃離。 莎莉：就像一句古老諺語說的：「我不用跑得比熊快，我只需要跑得比你快。」 哈莉特：真有趣，妳真是一個喜劇演員。 一個毛毛的小白球突然地飛奔越過營地，並且消失在叢林的另一邊裡。莎莉和蒂娜尖叫，然後跑去躲在帳篷裡。這一個毛茸茸的小動物慢慢地從叢林裡跳向哈莉特。她開始大笑。 哈莉特：哇！它只是一隻小免子，它好可愛。 另外兩個女生從躲的地方出來看。 莎莉：那正是我們之前擔心的。我現在覺得很愚蠢。 蒂娜：我也是。這個小東西連跳蚤都不會去傷害。 在這群女生明白什麼事情發生之前，這隻小免子向她們撲過來，並且把他們撕成碎片。這是他們第一次，也是最後一次遇見罕見的「兔人」。
answers: It couldn’t have been made by a wolf because there are no wolves in Taiwan.
They want her to check it out because she is the slowest and they can get away if it attacks her.
They scream and run to hide in the tent.

3.title: The Wrong Bathroom
paras: Did you hear what Tom did this afternoon?
Yes, I heard that he went into the girls’ bathroom.
Really? Why did he do that? Was it on purpose?
I don’t think it was on purpose. He is just near-sighted.
Do you really believe that? It sounds like a poor excuse to me.
Maybe, he does look kind of creepy.
I agree with Betty. He is a creep.
You two are so judgmental. You don’t even know his side of the story. You haven’t even given the guy a chance to explain himself.
I guess you’re right. Maybe we should wait to hear the whole story before we jump to conclusions.
How would you like it if someone spread rumorsabout you?
I wouldn’t like it very much. Still, don’t you find it odd that, after going to the same school for 3 years, he would “accidentally” go into the girls’ washroom?
You do have a point. That is weird.
Maybe he just had to go really badly and didn’t think he could make it to the boys’ washroom. Think about it. What would you rather do: a) pee yourself or b) use the boys’ washroom?
Since you put it that way…
Look! Here he comes now. Linda waves him over.
Yes?
We were just wondering why you went into the girls’ bathroom. Can you tell us?

Sure I can tell you. You see… Hey! Look over there! The girls look behind them to see what Tom is pointing at. Meanwhile he makes a break for it 16 When the girls turn back around, they realize they’ve been tricked.
I’ve changed my mind. He’s a creep.
And I guess we’ll never know the truth.
I guess so.

3.translation: 下課後，一些女生們正在聊她們學校的一個男生。 琳達：你有聽說今天下午湯姆做了什麼嗎？ 法蘭欣：有，我聽說他進去女生廁所。 貝蒂：真的嗎？他為什麼做那種事呢？他是故意的嗎？ 琳達：我認為不是故意的，他只是近視吧。 法蘭欣：你真的相信是那樣嗎？對我來說，這聽起來像是一個很爛的藉口。 貝蒂：也許，他看起來的確有點詭異。 法蘭欣：我同意貝蒂說的，他是一個很詭異的人。 琳達：你們兩個都太急著評斷別人了。你們甚至還不知道他自己的說法。你們甚至沒有給他為自己解釋的機會。 貝蒂：我想你講的沒錯。也許我們在驟下結論前，應該先聽聽全部的情況。 琳達：如果有人散播關於你的謠言，妳感受如何？ 法蘭欣：我不會很喜歡。然而，在同樣的學校讀了三年之後，他會「意外地」走進女生廁所，你不覺得這很古怪嗎？ 貝蒂：你講的的確有理，那很奇怪。 琳達：也許他只是真的很急，而且認為他無法忍到男生廁所。想想看，你比較願意怎麼做：a) 尿在褲子或 b) 使用男生廁所？ 法蘭欣：既然你那樣說…… 貝蒂：你看！現在他來了。 琳達揮手叫他過來。 湯姆：什麼事？ 琳達：我們剛想知道為什麼昨天你進去女生廁所。你可以告訴我們嗎？ 湯姆：當然我可以告訴你們。你們看……嘿！看那邊！ 女生們看身後湯姆指的地方，在此同時他溜走了。當女生們轉頭回來時，她們發現被他戲弄了。 琳達：我已經改變我的心意了。他是個詭異的人。 法蘭欣：我想我們將永遠不會知道真相。 貝蒂：我猜是的。
answers: She thought he went into the wrong bathroom because he was nearsighted.
She called them judgmental because they judged Tom before hearing his side of the story.
He tricked the girls and ran away to avoid telling them the truth.

3.title: Stressed About Tests
paras: So are you ready for the big test?
Not really. I don’t think I’m strong enough in Science. There is no way I can pass.
How about you, Jock?
I’m not ready for the math test. I find math so difficult even though I practice it every day.
Well…I’m worried about every subject. I don’t think I could even pass a urine test.
I’m so anxious about the whole thing that I haven’t been able to sleep for the past week.
Wow. That is bad. At least I can sleep. Have you been having any stress related problems, Jock?
Not anything too bad, but I have been biting my fingernails more than usual.
I’ll take biting my fingernails over not sleeping anytime. While they are talking outside, their favorite teacher, Mr. Lin, comes up to talk to them.
What’s up, boys?
We’re just talking about the tests and how worried we are.
Don’t worry too much about it. You can only do your best. Henry (frantically): What if I fail? What if I fail?!
If you fail, you can do a re-write in July. That wasn’t even an option when I was your age.
And if we fail the re-write?
You aren’t going to fail, so stop worrying about it. And even if you do, the sun will still come up in the morning.
No, I’m pretty sure my mother would make sure that it wouldn’t.
Just tell me one thing. How can I sleep at night until this is finally over?

Have a nice hot bath, a warm glass of milk and turn your mind off. There is no sense worrying about something you can’t control. Besides, it is very important to get sleep because your brain is like a muscle and it needs rest, too. Studying too much can be counterproductive, so find time to rest and you will increase your chance of passing. Taking a test when you are rested is far better than taking a test when you are all worried and stressed out.
Well… Our rides are here, so we have to go. Thanks for your advice.
Remember… Lots of rest and don’t over-study; especially the night before.
We won’t. Thanks again.

3.translation: 一些學生正站在學校外，等待著父母來接他們。他們正在討論著即將到來的基本學力測驗。 哈利：你準備好參加大考了嗎？ 亨利：還沒完全準備好。我認為我的理化不夠強，不可能會過關的。 哈利：喬克，那你呢？ 喬克：我數學還沒準備好。我覺得數學很難，雖然我每天都練習算數學。 哈利：嗯……我每一科都擔心。我認為我甚至連尿液檢查都不會過關。 亨利：我因為考試緊張到上個星期都睡不著。 哈利：哇，真是糟糕！至少我睡得著。喬克，你有壓力相關的問題嗎？ 喬克：情況沒有太糟啦，但是我最近咬指甲的次數比平常還多。 亨利：我寧願咬指甲而不願無法入眠。 當他們正在外面講話時，他們最喜愛的林老師走向前跟他們說話。 林老師：男孩們怎麼啦？ 男孩們：我們正在討論著考試以及我們有多擔心。 林老師：不要太擔心。你們只能盡力而為。 亨利（激動著說）：萬一我沒考過？萬一沒過呢？！ 林老師：假如你沒過，你七月可以重考啊。當我在你們這個年紀的時候根本沒有這種機會。 喬克：假如我們重考又失利呢？ 林老師：你們將不會落榜，所以就別再擔心了。假如你們真的落榜了，早上的太陽一樣會升起啊。 哈利：不，我很肯定我媽一定確信它不會升起。 亨利：只要告訴我一件事。一直到考試最後結束前，我晚上要怎樣才睡得著？ 林老師：洗個熱水澡，喝一杯溫牛奶，然後把腦袋放空。擔心你無法控制的事情是沒有意義的。此外，睡眠充足是很重要的，因為你的頭腦就像肌肉一樣也需要休息。唸太多書反而會導致反效果，所以，找時間休息，那麼就可以提高你上榜的機會。在充分休息的情況下考試，遠比在擔心及壓力沉重時考試來得好。 男孩們：嗯……我們的車子來了，所以我們得走了。謝謝您的建議。 林老師：記得喔……要充分休息，不要唸過頭了，尤其是在考試的前一晚。 男孩們：我們不會的。再次感謝您！
answers: He can’t sleep because he is too anxious about the upcoming exams.
He has been biting his fingernails.
He thinks that the brain is like a muscle and needs rest, too.

3.title: Waiting for Departure
paras: Excuse me. Is this Gate “C”?
Yes. Are you going to Japan, too?
Yes, I am.
Oh. Is it for business or pleasure?
Actually, I live there. How about you?
I have to catch a connecting flight there. I’m actually going to Vancouver.
How long is your layover going to be?
About 1. hours or so, but I have a few last-minute things to get at the duty-free shops anyway.
Were you here in Malaysia on business?
No, I’m just transferring here. I was in Australia visiting a cousin. And yourself?
I came here on vacation.
And how was it?
I had a really nice time: Good food, nice beaches, fantastic shopping. Malaysia has a little bit of everything to offer. But I did have trouble going through security.
I hate going through security and customs, too. What happened?
I guess I look suspicious or something. They took me aside and searched my carry-on bag. Then they took me into a little room and gave me a strip search.
Seriously? That sounds terrible. Are you alright?
Yeah, I’m fine, but it was absolutely humiliating.
Well, that does answer one question I was going to ask you?
What was that?
Where are your pants? Mr. Bean’s face turns beet red and he runs for the bathroom.

3.translation: 兩個陌生人在馬來西亞的國際機場開始聊起來了賓先生：請問一下，這是 C登機門嗎？ 瓊斯先生：是啊，你也要去日本嗎？ 賓先生：是的，沒錯。 瓊斯先生：喔，你是要出差還是去玩呢？ 賓先生：事實上我住日本。那你呢？ 瓊斯先生：我必須去那裡趕搭接駁航班。事實上，我要去溫哥華。 賓先生：那你臨時滯留的時間會多久呢？ 瓊斯先生：大約一個半小時，但是反正我還要去免稅店商品趕買一些東西。 賓先生：你是到馬來西亞出差的嗎？ 瓊斯先生：不，我只是在這裡轉機。我去澳洲探訪一位表姊。你呢？ 賓先生：我來這裡渡假。 瓊斯先生：你的假期如何呢？ 賓先生：我真的玩得很開心：食物很好吃、沙灘很美、購物很愉快。馬來西亞在各方面都有好玩的地方，但是在通過安檢時我確實碰到了麻煩。 瓊斯先生：我也很討厭通過安檢和海關。你發生了什麼事？ 賓先生：我想我看起來很可疑或什麼的。他們帶我到一旁搜我的隨身行李，然後，他們帶我進去一個小房間脫衣搜身。 瓊斯先生：真的嗎？好可怕啊！你沒事吧？ 賓先生：是的，我沒事。但是真的是受盡羞辱。 瓊斯先生：你正好回答了我想問你的一個問題。 賓先生：那是什麼？ 瓊斯先生：你的褲子在哪裡？ 賓先生的臉漲得通紅，並衝進洗手間。
answers: He was there visiting his cousin.
He is going to do some last-minute shopping at the duty-free shops.
He forgot to put them on after the strip search.

3.title: Thinking about Egypt
paras: So, I was thinking about taking a trip this week?
Where are you thinking about going?
I haven’t made up my mind yet. Do you have any suggestions?
Well… It is winter, so you should go south where it will be warmer. How about Kenting?
Actually, I was thinking about something overseas;3 Maybe Egypt.
That’s kind of far for a spur-of-the-moment vacation.
I know, but I’ve always wanted to see the pyramids.
Me, too. They are so amazing and mysterious.
And I want to see a mummy, too.
Not me. Mummies scare me. I find them creepy.
What about the Sphinx?
I wouldn’t mind seeing that. It would be awesome to actually say that I’ve stood next to something so ancient.
I guess that I have made up my mind then. Egypt it is.
Great! Can I come along?
Sorry, but you would cramp my style.
What? Cramp YOUR style? That’s no way to talk to a friend.
But what if I met a nice girl and wanted to spend time with her? You would be like a third wheel.
You’re just going to Egypt to pick up girls, aren’t you?
I wouldn’t say JUST to pick up girls, although it is high on my list of priorities.
It’s pretty sad when you have to leave the country to find a girl that will date you.

You’re so not funny. Maybe I should take you along and have you mummified. That way when an archaeologist digs you up in a thousand years, someone might actually be interested in what you have to say.

3.translation: 傑瑞德：我在想這星期去旅行。 肯特：你想要去哪裡呢？ 傑瑞德：我還沒有拿定主意。你有任何建議嗎？ 肯特：嗯……現在是冬天，你應該要去南方，那裡會比較溫暖。去墾丁如何？ 傑瑞德：事實上，我想的是國外的地方，可能是埃及。 肯特：對於一趟心血來潮的旅行來說，埃及是有點遠。 傑瑞德：我知道，但是我一直以來都想要去看看金字塔。 肯特：我也是。它們令人感到驚奇，而且非常的神祕。 傑瑞德：我也想要去看看木乃伊。 肯特：我不要。我會怕木乃伊，我覺得他們很恐怖。 傑瑞德：那獅身人面如何呢？ 肯特：我不介意去看那個。若真能說我曾經站在這麼古老的建築物旁邊，這就太棒了。 傑瑞德：我想我已經做好決定了，就是埃及。 肯特：太棒了！我可以一起去嗎？ 傑瑞德：抱歉，你將會降低我的風格。 肯特：啥？降低「你的」風格？對朋友不該講這種話的。 傑瑞德：但如果我遇到了一個好女孩，並且想和她在一起的話怎麼辦？你會像是個電燈泡一樣。 肯特：你只是想要去埃及把馬子而已，不是嗎？ 傑瑞德：我不會說「只是」去把馬子，雖然那是我列為優先考慮要做的前幾項之一。 肯特：若你必須出國去找一個願意和你約會的女孩，這真是很悲哀的事。 傑瑞德：你真的很沒有幽默感。也許我應該帶你一起去，然後把你變成木乃伊。這樣一來，一千年以後當考古學家把你挖起來時，或許會有人對於你要說的話感到興趣。
answers: He suggests that Jared should go where it is warmer.
He wants to see the pyramids, the Sphinx and girls.
He thinks that Kent would cramp his style

3.title: Woman’s Work
paras: What does your father do?
He works in an office.
That is pretty general. Lots of people work in an office. Can you be more specific?
He works in the accounting department at the Pretend Bicycle Company factory.
So he is an accountant?
Not exactly. He takes phone calls, schedules meetings, and takes care of incoming mail.
Oh! He is a secretary.
He prefers to be called an office assistant. He gets mad when people call him a secretary. He says only women are called secretaries.
I see.
And what does your dad do?
Don’t laugh, but he is a nurse. Jessica (laughing): Sorry. It just sounds funny to call a man a nurse. Annie (frowning angrily): I told you not to laugh. At least my dad isn’t a secretary!
Again, I’m sorry. It is funny, though, that both of our dads have “women’s” jobs.
I wonder if their friends tease them about it.
Probably just behind their backs. The girls snicker at the thought of their dads being teased like school boys.

3.translation: 安妮跟她的朋友潔西卡正在一間網咖裡面聊天…。 安妮：你爸爸是從事什麼樣的工作啊？ 潔西卡：他在辦公室上班。 安妮：你這樣子說太籠統了。有很多人都是在辦公室上班，你可以說得具體一點嗎？ 潔西卡：他是在普利天腳踏車公司工廠的會計部門工作。 安妮：那麼他是個會計師囉？ 潔西卡：不完全是。他負責接聽電話、安排開會，以及打理接收郵件。 安妮：噢！那他是個秘書囉！ 潔西卡：他比較喜歡大家稱他為辦公室助理。每次大家稱他為秘書時他就會生氣，他說只有女人才會被稱為秘書。 安妮：了解。 潔西卡：那你爸爸是從事什麼工作呢？ 安妮：說了你不要笑，他是個護士。 潔西卡：(笑出來)抱歉，只是稱一個男人為護士聽起來很好笑。 安妮：(生氣地皺眉)我告訴過你不要笑的，至少我爸爸不是個秘書！ 潔西卡：再次感到抱歉。然而，我們的爸爸都是從事「女人的職業」，這真好笑。 安妮：我很好奇他們的朋友會不會取笑他們的工作。 潔西卡：或許只會在他們的背後說吧！ 這兩個女孩一想到他們的爸爸像學生一樣被嘲弄時，不禁暗地笑了起來。
answers: He works in the accounting department at the Pretend Bicycle Company factory.
He thinks only women are called secretaries.
She gets angry because she told Jessica not to laugh, but she did anyway.

4.title: Tickets for the Big Show
paras: What are you up to this weekend?
I was thinking about going to the concert in Taichung.
Who is playing?
Jolin is doing a show at the baseball park.
Really? If she is coming, I would’ve thought it would be sold out by now.
It is, but I have a friend who has tickets and can’t go.
How many tickets does he have?
He told me 4, but 2 are already spoken for.
That leaves 2: 1 for you and 1 for me.
Not so fast. I wanted to ask Colleen to come with me.
You wouldn’t deny me the chance to see Jolin live, would you?
I know you are a big fan, but Colleen is much better looking than you. Sorry.

A big fan? I am her biggest fan. I have every album she has ever made and every DVD, too. It would crush me if I didn’t get to see her. You know I haven’t had the chance before, right? Thomas (sigh of pity): I know. I know. Ok, you can have the tickets and I’ll take Colleen to the movies instead. Kenny (tries to hug Thomas): Thank you. Thank you. Thank you. Thomas (defending against the hug): Alright! I got it. Just stop with the hugs. Jeez.
Ok. How much are the tickets?
My friend said they are 6000… Kenny (reaches for his wallet): No problem.
Each! Kenny (stops taking his wallet out): On second thought, maybe I’ll just wait for the Live DVD. Thomas (shaking his head): Some fan you are.

4.translation: 肯尼：你這個週末計畫做什麼呢？ 湯瑪斯：我想到台中聽演唱會。 肯尼：誰要演出呢？ 湯瑪斯：蔡依林要在棒球場做一場演出。 肯尼：真的嗎？如果她要來，我想現在入場券早就銷售一空了。 湯瑪斯：沒錯，我有一個朋友他有票，但是他沒辦法去。 肯尼：他有多少張票呢？ 湯瑪斯：他告訴我有4張票，但是已經有兩張被人訂走了， 肯尼：那還有兩張，一張給你，一張給我。 湯瑪斯：別這麼快就下結論，我是想邀請卡琳跟我一起去看。 肯尼：你不會拒絕給我這個機會去看蔡依林的現場演出吧？ 湯瑪斯：我知道你是他的忠心歌迷，但是卡琳比你好看多了。不好意思！ 肯尼：忠心的歌迷而已？我是她最忠心的歌迷，我有她曾經製作的每一張專輯和DVD。如果沒辦法去看她表演會讓我心碎的。你知道我以前從沒有這個機會的，不是嗎？ 湯瑪斯 (同情的嘆息)：我知道，我知道！好吧，票可以給你，而我帶卡琳去看電影好了。 肯尼 (試著要給湯馬斯一個擁抱)：謝謝！謝謝！謝謝你！ 湯瑪斯 (拒絕給他擁抱)：是的，我知道了！不要擁抱了，天哪！ 肯尼：好吧！那票要多少錢呢？ 湯瑪斯：我的朋友說要6000元。 肯尼 (伸手去拿他的皮夾)：沒問題！ 湯瑪斯：每一張票喔！ 肯尼 (拿皮夾的動作停下來)：在重新思考後，或許我應該等她現場演唱的DVD發行。 湯瑪斯 (搖頭)：你這個歌迷真是不怎麼樣！
answers: He had a friend who had tickets, but couldn’t go.
He wanted to see the show badly because he is Jolin’s biggest fan.
The price of the tickets changed his mind about going.

4.title: The Shark’s Plight
paras: Ok class. Today we’re going to see how much you know about sharks.
Cool. That sounds fun.
First of all, what is the difference between a shark and all other fish?
I know! Sharks are bigger.
Yes, some sharks are bigger, but some are smaller, too. Anyone else?
Sharks don’t have any bones.
That is right, Frank. Sharks don’t have bones. Their skeletal structure is made entirely of cartilage just like your ear.
And sharks are the only fish that kill people.

That’s not true. Some fish kill people because they have poisonous spines. Really, though, sharks aren’t as dangerous as most people believe. Let me ask you this: How many people are attacked by sharks worldwide every year?
I think about 1, are attacked every year.
Wow, that’s a lot and very specific, but it’s wrong. How about you, Sonia? What do you think?
I think it is probably closer to 5000.
Actually, on average, between 50 and 70 people are attacked by sharks.
That’s not very many.
No, it isn’t. Why do you think Sonia and Jeffery guessed such a high number?
Because people are very afraid of sharks and there are lots of documentaries on TV about shark attacks.

That’s right. People have a strange relationship with sharks; they are afraid of them, but they love to watch TV shows about them. The reality is more people are killed by bees or struck by lightning every year, but movies like Jaws and other horror films have created a great fear of sharks in people. Of the people attacked, how many actually die, Tony?
Probably around 12.
Yes, about 8 to 12 people die every year from shark attacks. What country reports the most shark attacks every year?
Australia.

Many people say that, but the USA is actually number 1. Most attacks are in Florida. Surprisingly, if you are attacked there, the chances of it being fatal are almost 0. This is because there is a shark “nursery” in the area, so most shark attacks in Florida involve inquisitive young sharks that aren’t big enough to cause much damage.
What kind of sharks are the most dangerous?
The Great White Shark.
Yes, that is one. What are the other two?
The Whale Shark.
That’s crazy! Whale sharks are one of the most passive creatures in the ocean. Plus, they don’t have teeth.
The tiger shark and the bull shark?
Yes, they are the other 2. What makes the bull shark exceptionally different from most other sharks?
It can live for long periods of time in fresh water.
Yes, Alan. How did you know that?
Learning about sharks is one of my hobbies.
Good for you. That is a great hobby.
Did you know that bull sharks have been found hundreds of km’s upstream in many rivers?
I didn’t know that. Thanks, Alan.
I heard that people kill many sharks every year just for their fins.
Yes, but how many? Do you know?
Not even close.
5 million.
Yes, if you multiply that number by 20. The answer is over 100, 000, 000 sharks are slaughtered every year.
That’s terrible. What can we do to help the sharks?
Don’t buy shark meat, shark fin soup or any pills that contain shark byproducts.
Why do people take shark pills?
It’s because shark cartilage helps people with joint trouble. It is kind of like taking glucosamine.
Yeah. My grandpa takes them for arthritis. I told him about the shark killings, but he doesn’t care.
Well…All we can do is to inform people and make them aware of the sharks’ plight. And pray!

4.translation: 生物課的學生正在談論鯊魚及鯊魚的攻擊事件。 老師：好的，班上同學。今天我們要看你們對鯊魚的了解有多少。 學生：太棒了！聽起來蠻有趣的。 老師：首先，鯊魚和其他種類的魚差別在哪裡？ 傑克：我知道！鯊魚比較大。 老師：沒錯！有的鯊魚比較大，但也有的鯊魚比較小。其他人有答案嗎？ 法蘭克：鯊魚沒有任何的骨頭。 老師：是的！法蘭克。鯊魚沒有骨頭。他們的骨架結構全由軟骨構成的，就像你的耳朵一樣。 湯瑪士：鯊魚是唯一會殺人的魚。 老師：這不是真的。有一些魚會殺人，因為他們的刺是有毒的。然而說真的，鯊魚並沒有像大部分人們認為的那麼的危險。讓我來問你們這個問題：全世界每年有多少人被鯊魚攻擊？ 傑佛瑞：我認為每年約有1523人被攻擊。 老師：哇！這很多，而且數字很明確，但是這是錯誤的。你覺得怎樣？索尼婭， 妳認為呢？ 索尼婭：我想可能接近5000吧！ 老師：事實上，每年平均約有50到70的人被鯊魚攻擊過。 米雪兒：這不多啊！ 老師：對啊！不多。你認為為什麼索尼婭和傑佛瑞會猜那麼高的數字呢？ 山姆：因為大家都很怕鯊魚，而且有很多關於鯊魚攻擊的紀錄片在電視上播報。 老師：沒錯！人們跟鯊魚的關係很奇怪，他們害怕鯊魚，但又喜歡看鯊魚的電視節目。事實是這樣的，每年有更多人是被蜜蜂蜇死，或是被雷擊打死的。但像是大白鯊的電影及其它恐怖片已經使人們產生對於鯊魚產生很大的恐懼。而在被攻擊的人當中，真正有多少人喪生呢，湯尼？ 湯尼：大約有12個人左右。 老師：是的，每年大約8~12人死於鯊魚的攻擊。哪個國家每年有最多鯊魚攻擊事件的報告呢? 蜜雪兒：澳洲。 老師：很多人都會這樣子說，但事實上美國是第一名。大部分攻擊是在佛羅里達州。令人感到驚訝的是，假使你在那裡被攻擊，那死亡機率幾乎是零。 因為那裡是鯊魚養殖場，所以在佛羅里達州多數鯊魚攻擊事件都是與好奇的小鯊魚有關，由於牠們還太小，不至於造成很大的傷害。 亨利：最危險的是什麼鯊魚？ 艾倫：大白鯊。 老師：對，這是其中一種，還有另外兩種呢？ 安：鯨鯊老師：那太瘋狂了！鯨鯊是海洋上最溫馴的生物之一，此外，牠們並沒有牙齒。 湯尼：是虎鯊跟公牛鯊? 老師：是的，牠們就是另外的那兩種。公牛鯊特別和其它大多數的鯊魚不同的地方是甚麼呢? 艾倫：公牛鯊可以長時間在淡水中生活。 老師：沒錯，艾倫。你怎麼知道的？ 艾倫：學習鯊魚的相關知識是我的嗜好之一。 老師：很好，那是一個很棒的嗜好喔! 艾倫：你知道嗎，人們在許多河流發現公牛鯊逆游數百公里呢。 老師：我不知道，謝謝你，艾倫。 貝絲：我聽說人們為了鯊魚的鰭，每年殺死很多鯊魚。 老師：是的，但有多少？你知道嗎？ 蘇尼亞：5000嗎? 老師：差太多了。 山姆：5百萬。 老師：是的，如果你們把這數字乘上20，答案就是每年有超過1億的鯊魚被宰殺。 提姆：那太糟糕了，我們該如何幫助鯊魚？ 老師：不要買鯊魚肉、魚翅湯或任何有鯊魚副產品的藥丸。 蘇尼亞：為什麼人們要吃鯊魚藥丸? 老師：因為鯊魚軟骨有助於解決人們關節的問題，有點像是吃葡萄糖氨。 艾倫：沒錯。我祖父因為關節炎而服用它們。我告訴他關於屠殺鯊魚的事情，      但他並不在乎。 老師：嗯，我們所能做的只有為大家提供資訊，讓他們了解到鯊魚的困境，並且禱告。
answers: They have cartilage.
It is used to make pills for various joint problems.
I can stop buying things made from or of sharks and inform people of the shark’s plight.

4.title: Endangered Sea Turtles
paras: What are you reading about, Chuck?
I’m reading about Green Sea Turtles. It’s a kind of sea turtle.
Oh. You mean like the Loggerhead Sea Turtle?
Yes. Do you like sea turtles?
I guess, but I don’t know much about them.
I can teach you about them.
Ok. Tell me about them.
Did you know that many sea turtles are endangered?
Actually, I did. I also heard that many different species used to lay their eggs on the beaches of Taiwan.
Yes, but the green turtle is the only one that still does.
Why is that?
Well, before 1992 Taiwan didn’t do anything to protect their nesting areas and a lot of sites were lost because of urbanization.
Do they protect them now?
Yes, but only few spots on the surrounding islands of Taiwan remain.
Is that the main reason their populations have declined.
Really, it is much more horrifying than that. People catch them for their meat, their shells (carapaces) are used to make decorations, and some people even try to keep them as pets.
My grandpa told me that some temples buy them and put them on display.
That is strange considering that in Chinese culture they are a symbol of luck, prosperity and longevity.
I know. You’d think people would want to protect them because of cultural importance.
I guess the truth is that many people here are just not informed about what is going on so the poaching continues.
Are other countries the same way?

I suppose a lot of countries are, but some are being very proactive like Costa Rica for example. My cousin went there for a wedding in February and he said that the turtle nesting grounds were fenced off. They even had guards patrolling the area to make sure that no one gets in.
It’s good to know that some places are taking action.
It sure is and hopefully our country and others will start doing more as well.
I’m going to go home and look on the internet. Maybe there are some volunteer jobs available so that I can do my part.
That’s a great idea. Let me know if you find anything and I will sign up, too.
Great! I’ll catch ya later.
Alright. Later, man.

4.translation: 達西：達西你在讀什麼書？ 查克：我正在閱讀有關於綠蠵龜的書，這是一種海龜。 達西：喔！你的意思是說像是蠵龜的海龜嗎？ 查克：是的，你喜歡海龜嗎？ 達西：我想是吧！但是我對牠們並沒有很多的了解。 查克：我可以敎你相關知識。 達西：好！你就告訴我吧。 查克：你知道很多的海龜已經瀕臨絕種了嗎？ 達西：事實上我知道。我也聽說很多不同種類的海龜以前在台灣的海灘下蛋。 查克：是的，但是現在只剩下綠蠵龜還會在這裡下蛋。 達西：為什麼會這樣呢？ 查克：嗯，在1992年之前，台灣並沒有任何作為來保護牠們築巢的區域，而且很多的築巢區都因為都市化而不見了。 達西：現在有保護牠們嗎？ 查克：是的，但是只有在台灣離島的一些地點還保留。 達西：這就是牠們的數量會下降的主要原因。 查克：事實比這個更恐怖。人們抓海龜來吃牠們的肉，或是用牠們的殼來做裝飾品，而甚至有些人把牠們當寵物養。 達西：我的祖父告訴我，有一些廟宇買海龜，並且拿牠們來展示。 查克：這就奇怪了，在中國文化中牠們是好運、繁榮、長壽的象徵。 達西：我知道，你會認為人們會想要保護海龜，因為牠們在文化上的重要性。 查克：我想事實上是這樣的，這邊很多人對海龜的現況並不知情，所以盜獵的行為仍然持續中。 達西：其他的國家也相同嗎？ 查克：我想很多的國家也是這樣的，但是有些像哥斯大黎加的國家就預先採取行動來保護牠們。我的表弟二月份到那裡舉行一場婚禮，他說：在烏龜的築巢地都被用籬笆圍起來。他們甚至還有警衛巡邏這個區域，以確保沒有人會進來盜獵。 達西：知道有些地方有採取行動真好。 查克：的確是如此。但願我們的國家和其他國家也能多做一點事來保護海龜。 達西：我想要回家搜尋網路資訊，或許可以找到一些義工的工作，這樣我便可以盡一點心意。 查克：這是很棒的點子。如果你有找到任何義工的工作，要我知道，我也要報名。 達西：太棒了！等會兒見。 查克：好的老兄，待會兒見。
answers: No, they are endangered.
One of the main reasons is the loss of nesting sights due to human encroachment.
They have turned the turtles’ nesting sites into environmental sanctuaries.

4.title: Massive Earthquake Strikes Japan
paras: Did you hear about the catastrophe in Japan?
No, I didn’t. What happened?
There was a huge earthquake just off the coast. It measured (registered) 9 on the Richter scale.
9? That is the largest one I’ve ever heard of.
But that’s not the worst part.
Really? What else happened?

Because the earthquake was offshore, it caused a tsunami. The following swell went as far as 4 km’s inland some places. It capsized boats and some floated down streets. It washed away buildings, farms, and even entire villages. It was like a giant bulldozer leveled the place.
Are you serious?
The footage has been all over the news. I can’t believe you haven’t seen or heard of this yet.
Was there much damage?
Are you kidding? The cost is expected to be in the 10’s of billions of dollars.
That’s awful. Were many people killed?

The latest death toll was estimated at over 2000, but that is just confirmed deaths. The final numbers are probably going to be much, much higher because there are some towns that are completely destroyed.
I can’t even imagine how horrible people in Japan must feel right now.
I think they’re all probably still in shock.
That is such a tragedy. Is there anything we can do to help?
I’m not sure. Many countries have already offered aid and sent in relief workers to search for survivors and to help with first aid. There are probably organizations you can donate money to.
What will they do with the money?
It will be used to provide medical supplies, food, clothing to those affected by the disaster. Some of the money will also go to help rebuild the country.
I’m going to go home and talk my parents into sending some money. I want to do whatever I can.
Me, too. I think sending some money is the least we can do.

4.translation: 琴：你有聽到日本的大災難嗎？ 蒂娜：沒有，我沒聽到。是發生什麼事了？ 琴：距離海岸不遠處有一個大地震，它測出來是芮氏地震規模9級。 蒂娜：9級？這是我聽過最大的地震。 琴：但是，那不是最嚴重的部份。 蒂娜：真的！還發生什麼事？ 琴：因為地震發生在近海處，結果導致海嘯。隨後洶湧的海浪在某些地方沖入到內陸4公里處。它把船打翻，一些船沿著街道漂流。海水沖走了建築物、農田，甚至整個村莊。這種感覺就像是巨大的堆土機把這個地方剷平。 蒂娜：你是說真的嗎？ 琴：海嘯的影片在新聞到處都看得到。我簡直無法相信你到現在還沒有看到或聽到這一則消息。 蒂娜：受損很嚴重嗎？ 琴：你在開玩笑嗎？損失的金額預估有數百億美元。 蒂娜：太可怕了！很多人喪命嗎？ 琴：最新的死亡人數預估超過2000人，不過那只是證實的死亡人數。最後的死亡的人數可能會比現在高出非常多，因為有一些城鎮完全被摧毀。 蒂娜：我無法想像日本人現在的感受有多恐怖。 琴：我想他們到現在仍然還處於震驚當中。 蒂娜：這真是一場悲劇，有沒有什麼我們可以幫助他們的？ 琴：我不確定。很多國家已經提供援助，並且派遣救難人員去尋找倖存者，以及協助急救。或許有一些組織可以讓你捐款。 蒂娜：他們會拿這些錢來做什麼？ 琴：這些捐款將用來提供醫療物資、食物、衣物給那些受災難影響的災民。有一些錢也可以用來幫助這個國家的重建。 蒂娜：我現在要回家說服我的父母捐出一些錢。我想要做任何我可以幫忙的事。 琴：我也是。我想捐一些錢我們能做到的最低限度了。
answers: It struck 4 km’s off the coast of Japan.
It triggered a tsunami.
They are sending disaster relief aid.

4.title: Pondering Life in the Universe
paras: Do you ever wonder about life on other planets? 3
All the time.
Do you think there is intelligent life somewhere else in the universe?

The universe is so incomprehensibly big that I can’t imagine there not being something out there. Besides, there must be some intelligent life up there because there isn’t any down here. (Laughs at his own little joke)
I can’t even comprehend the size of our own solar system, let alone our galaxy or the entire universe.
Few can, but I guess the real question is: Do you believe in aliens?
Yeah, they must be out there somewhere.
Do you think they have visited us before?
It is hard to ignore millions of people all around the world that have claimed to have seen UFO’s.
Why haven’t the aliens ‘officially’ come out of the closet—so to speak—and made a public appearance then?
Maybe they are just observing us and doing experiments.
I heard about a guy in Jong Jing that says he was abducted by aliens. He says they took him out of his bed in the middle of the night, but that is all he remembers.
Reports of alien abductions aren’t that uncommon. There are even credible people that have claimed to be taken aboard alien ships… or should I say, formerly credible.
Why do you think they are studying us?
Probably the same reason humans study everything they can get their hands on.
And why is that?
Our curiosity. People are curious about everything and are always doing experiments or studying something, so why would aliens be any different?
I guess so, but I still don’t understand the need for secrecy.
I would say because they know us all too well. If people knew there were really aliens, they would probably try to start a war with them or something like that.
Right, again! The human race certainly doesn’t have a very good track record when it comes to peaceful negotiations.
Anyway, I’d better get home before my mom starts to wonder if I’ve been abducted by aliens.
Me, too. I’ll catch you later.
Bye!
